blurbs.blurb: A volcanic Imperial world until Dark Mechanicum forges beneath its crust were unmasked. Warpsmiths triggered eruptions that loosed hordes of daemon engines, ending all human life within a month.
An uncharted point in Segmentum Obscurus, cataloged only as Abyss. No further record survives in the archive.
An Imperial forge world on the fringe of the Ork empire of Charadon, home to the Titans of Legio Destructor. Its magi claim kinship with Ultramar, though Mars ranks the world low among the forges.
Once an Imperial holding in Segmentum Tempestus, the Adransa Cluster fell to Warboss Dregdakka's Waaagh! in 928.M41 after Imperial forces lost the disastrous stand at Thrassos. Orks hold it still.
A hyper-advanced human world isolated from the Imperium for five millennia. Lord Commander Solar Macharius took it at ruinous cost, then a redirected comet erased its wonders and left a dead world.
A daemon world bound to Tzeentch's will in Segmentum Obscurus. The archive holds little beyond its name and allegiance.

An Imperial world whose pigment wells once yielded the STC pattern for Substance 145XX22, sparking a workers' rebellion crushed by an Imperial compliance force. Genestealer cults now riddle its population.
A forge world near the Eye of Terror known as the Orb of a Thousand Scars, chief armorer of the Cadian Gate. Its Titans of Legio Praesidium Vortex have repelled repeated Black Crusade assaults.
An unclassified point on Imperial star charts in Segmentum Ultima. Its record in the archive ends there.
A Knight World whose noble houses turned to the Ruinous Powers, now ruled by Chaos Knights from their dread keeps. Cultists and fallen nobles vie to raise the grandest shrines to Chaos.
A Necron tomb world in Segmentum Tempestus, its dynasty sealed away in stasis crypts since the fall of the old empire. Should it wake, whatever slumbers below will rise to reclaim the surface.
A Knight World known for its Sacred Isle, the seat around which the domains of its knightly houses are arrayed. Harsh, ancient law codes have seen two thirds of the population imprisoned or exiled.
A civilized world of the Imperium in Segmentum Obscurus, its population and industry left to develop without any singular Imperial purpose. Little more is recorded.
A warp storm churning through Segmentum Ultima, one more scar of the immaterium's tides. No further account of it survives.
An Imperial world in Segmentum Tempestus whose Astra Militarum regiments marched in the Indomitus Crusade. A genestealer cult has taken root beneath its recruiting grounds.
One of the cardinal worlds anchoring the Obolis Sub-Sector, a seat of ecclesiarchal power in Segmentum Ultima.
An Ork empire ruled by a line of Warchiefs, whose armies pushed the War of Dakka against the Tau Farsight Enclaves before their defeat in single combat.
A craftworld pulled into the Eye of Terror soon after the Fall of the Eldar. Its people endured ten millennia within the warp until Phoenix Lord Maugan Ra led them out during the 13th Black Crusade.
A Questor Mechanicus stronghold in Segmentum Ultima, home to Knight houses rather than forge industry. Fire-breathing Heldrake packs once ravaged its surface until Princeps Adalace burned them out.

An Imperial fortress and war world in Segmentum Ultima. In M42 the Iron Warriors under Warsmith Malek Ugor laid siege to it for years, halted only yards from the gubernatorial capital by the Crimson Fists.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima where Belisarius Cawl's excavations for blackstone tore open a hidden Necron tomb, turning a mining dispute into a galaxy spanning war.
A webway gate charted in Segmentum Tempestus, recorded in the census with little else known of it.
A minor forge and Titan world watching over the Maelstrom Zone for the Adeptus Mechanicus. Loyal to Ryza and garrisoned by Legio Crucius, its Magos stayed neutral through the Badab War.
A world given over to a secret Ordo Xenos research station studying captured Tyranids. When containment failed in 850.M41 the specimens broke loose and consumed the facility's garrison.
A hive world in Segmentum Ultima where a Khorne worshipping cult turned on the Imperium in late M38. The Bloodthirster An'ggrath answered their summons and annihilated every soul on the planet.

A dead world near the Veiled Region in Segmentum Tempestus. A Nurgle plague uprising forced an Exterminatus in M41, yet fighting continued into the age of the Great Rift with the Legion of the Damned lending aid.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, noted in the archive but otherwise undocumented.
A system in Segmentum Obscurus where Harakoni and Elysian regiments were sent in 997.M41 after contact was lost and the space hulk Agony of the Damned was sighted nearby.

A forge world raised within the Realm of Ultramar during the Great Crusade, long at odds with its sister world Konor. Its armoured companies bought time for the Ultramarines to retreat at the Battle of Calth.

A forge world capable of rivaling Mars in output, famed for casting Mars-Alpha pattern Warlord Titans. It tore itself apart in civil war during the Horus Heresy and later raised Legio Vulpa against Jindarii raiders.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Solar, its record in the Imperial archive is little more than a name and a coordinate.

A forge world in Segmentum Tempestus, named for giant arachnid megafauna hunted to extinction there. It defied Imperial rule during the Great Crusade and later fractured in a civil war that split its Titan Legion.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, remembered chiefly as the ground where the Catachan XXIV broke an Ork warband under Warlord Krakskull in 930.M41.
A webway gate bound into Aeldari myth, named for Eldanesh, the greatest of the mortal Eldar heroes. It marks one of the ancient crossings the Aeldari still guard within the webway's tunnels.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, catalogued but otherwise unremarked in the archive.
An Imperial Knight world of Segmentum Obscurus, its ruling households cut off from the wider Imperium since the opening of the Great Rift. Little of its current fate reaches Imperial record-keepers.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, present in the census with nothing further recorded.
A homeworld of the Adeptus Astartes in Segmentum Ultima, bound to the Chapter it raises and supplies.

An artificial world in the Chalnath Expanse, Segmentum Ultima, riddled with Genestealer Cult infestation beneath its guildhouses. T'au, Ork, and Imperial forces all fought there in the Chalnath campaigns.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its details absent from the surviving archive.

A forge world of Segmentum Pacificus known for the Artemia pattern Hellhound flame tank built in its manufactorums. During the Fourth Tyrannic War it was cut off by the Shadow in the Warp until relief arrived from the Sons of Medusa.

An Imperial research and monitoring station of Segmentum Obscurus, keeping watch over a lesser exit from the Eye of Terror. Abaddon the Despoiler's raid on its skeleton garrison in 139.M41 opened the Gothic War.
A former human hive world of Segmentum Ultima, conquered by the Necrons in 829.M41 and now the crownworld of the Nephrekh Dynasty, orbiting the three stars that give the dynasty its emblem.

A hive world of the Sabbat Worlds Cluster in Segmentum Pacificus. Chaos forces held its hive cities until an Imperial counteroffensive, backed by Legio Tempestus, purged them out over three months in 756 and 757.M41.
A civilized world of Segmentum Ultima that furnishes regiments to the Astra Militarum, its record in the archive otherwise sparse.
An Imperial cardinal world famed for its relics and wealth, invaded by the T'au during their Fifth Sphere Expansion while a genestealer cult festered unseen beneath its surface.
An agri world in Segmentum Ultima, its harvests entered in the Administratum's tithe grain but little else survives in the archive.
Settled by colonists from Phaeton during the Great Crusade, this forge world grew into the home of the Titan Legion Legio Atarus, among the first to turn against Horus.
A frigid knight world bound to the neighboring forge of Atar-Median, home to the Adeptus Mechanicus knight house Col'Khak.
An industrial world in Segmentum Obscurus that fills its manufactorum quotas while feeding regiments into the Astra Militarum.
Declared a war world and watched over by the Eldar, it was once defended from an Ork invasion by the Autarch Kael Ra.

A shadowed civilized world that unknowingly entombed the necron pirate king Thaszar the Invincible, whose awakening leveled its capital. Its Tunnel Rats regiments are prized urban fighters of the Guard.
An Imperial world remembered chiefly for a siege in which the 5th Valhalla tank company fought in squadron strength.
A feudal world of endless arid plains where tribal horse nomads roam beyond the single city of Khanasan, its warrior clans occasionally drawn into Imperial Guard service.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima, named but otherwise unrecorded in the archive.

Ruled by High Lord Gheret Rogge, this Imperial world in Segmentum Obscurus raises regiments for the Astra Militarum, including the 23rd Armoured sent to aid Commissar Yarrick's hunt for Ghazghkull Thraka.
A mineral-rich knight world tied closely to the neighboring forge of Bellus Prime, home to Knight House Vulker and its firepower-focused Tripartite Lance formation.
A verdant T'au resort world where retired elder heroes of the empire live out their years under heavy guard.
Built atop a necron tomb of the Sautekh dynasty, this knight world of the Eastern Fringe beat back a reawakening in 975.M41 through the strength of its three knightly houses.
A hive world invaded by the Iron Warriors during the Horus Heresy, its manufactorum stacks scarred by that early siege.
An industrial world lost to the Alpha Legion after Chaos sorcerers tricked Imperial cartographers into misplacing its system, sending the relief fleet to the wrong coordinates entirely.
A cardinal world northwest of the Eye of Terror, seat of Cardinal Armandus Helfire, remembered for a pilgrim uprising crushed by the Adepta Sororitas in the first combat use of the Repressor vehicle.

Built as a mirror of Prospero to shelter Thousand Sons scholarship, garrisoned by Imperial Fists and Custodes until the Legion teleported past its defenses in 012.M31 to recover forbidden knowledge from its capital pyramid.
Namesake of the Badab War, the largest Space Marine civil war since the Horus Heresy, sparked when Chapter Master Lufgt Huron styled himself Tyrant and led four Chapters into revolt.

The Segmentum Fortress of Segmentum Tempestus, a naval bastion of molten seas and armored basalt strongholds that coordinates the region's battlefleets, its garrisons since infiltrated by Genestealer cults.
A system on the fringe of Segmentum Obscurus where combined Blood Swords and Star Dragons fleets answered an Ordo Malleus summons to hunt down a daemon-possessed hulk known as the Accursed Eternity.
A war world of Segmentum Ultima, remembered only as one line among the many Imperial Guard battlegrounds cataloged in the Munitorum's wartime rolls.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, its details lost to the archive save for a bare listing in the sector census.

An icy, mineral-rich mining world of Segmentum Ultima, home to some twenty billion people and a quarter-billion-strong Imperial Guard garrison, kept under close Inquisitorial watch for the genestealer taint within its population.

A sparsely settled Imperial world on the galaxy's southern rim, flagged in Imperial records as genestealer-infested and once the site where Dark Angels forces broke a Crimson Slaughter warband hunting the relic called the Hellfire Stone.

Homeworld of the Exorcists Space Marine Chapter, an isolated feral world in the Narasima Straits scoured by acidic swamps. Its wary population, descended from stranded prospectors, has long forgotten off-world technology.
A civilized world of Segmentum Solar whose main duty to the Imperium is filling out fresh regiments of the Astra Militarum.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, logged in the census but otherwise absent from the archive.

An Imperial world in the western Ultima Segmentum that raises regiments for the Astra Militarum. Its three moons fell to the greenskins of Waaagh Grax during a mid-M41 invasion, though the planet itself held firm.

A Knight world of Segmentum Obscurus ruled by House Makabius, brought under Imperial rule only after its defeat by the Imperial Fists at Arben Moor. During the Horus Heresy the Household turned traitor and marched with the Death Guard at the Coronid Deeps.
A fortress world in Segmentum Ultima, one of the many armored bastions ringing Imperial space. The archive records little beyond its designation.
Homeworld of Knight House-019, a Mechanicus-aligned Knight House in the Thramas Sector bound by oath to the forge world Gulgorahd.
A fortress world of Segmentum Ultima, garrisoned against threats from beyond. No further record survives in the archive.
Another fortress world holding the line in Segmentum Ultima. Its garrison and history remain undocumented here.
An unclassified installation somewhere in Segmentum Ultima, its purpose never fully catalogued by Imperial cartographers.
An Imperial war world noted in the old regimental rosters of the Astra Militarum, otherwise lightly documented.
A world of Ultramar defended during the Great Crusade against Aeldari raiders, where Tetrarch Eikos Lamiad was grievously wounded and lost an eye.
The Imperial Navy fleet charged with guarding the Agripinaa Sector near the Eye of Terror, counted among the Bastion Fleets and battered during the Thirteenth Black Crusade.
The Imperial Navy fleet of the Armageddon Sector, driven into a costly fighting retreat against Ghazghkull's ork armada during the Third War for Armageddon.
The Imperial Navy fleet stationed at the Solari Anchorage above Metalica, largely broken when Typhus and the Death Guard fell upon the Charadon Sector.
An Imperial Navy fleet guarding the Belis Corona Sub-Sector in Segmentum Obscurus, one of the Bastion Fleets watching the Eye of Terror.
The Imperial Navy fleet of the Gothic Sector, which bore the brunt of Abaddon the Despoiler's Twelfth Black Crusade in the war later named for it.
A detachment split from Battlefleet Calixis to patrol the Koronus Passage and Expanse, contesting orks, pirates, and unknown xenos along the Halo Margins.
The segmentum fleet of Obscurus, kept in near constant motion by its proximity to the Cadian Gate and the ever present threat from the Eye of Terror.
The segmentum fleet of Pacificus, headquartered at Hydraphur and tasked with defending Imperial holdings across that stretch of the galaxy.
The Imperial Navy fleet charged with defending the Scarus Sector in Segmentum Obscurus, standing watch near the Eye of Terror against Abaddon's Black Crusades.
Guardian fleet of the Solar Sector and Terra itself, based at the Ring of Iron docks above Mars and ranked as the most senior of the Imperium's five battlefleets.
Naval force of the Ultima Segmentum, tasked with patrols, escort duty, and orbital support for Imperial Guard campaigns across that vast stretch of space.
A dead world converted into the Segmentum Obscurus's main naval dockyard, besieged by the Iron Warriors after the fall of Cadia despite tenacious Cadian defenders.
An unclassified world charted in Segmentum Ultima, its history absent from the wider archive.
Once home to the Emperor's Swords Chapter, wiped out to the last battle brother when a Necron tomb woke beneath the surface in 990.M41.
A forge world mined for ore and metal, defended by the Knights of House Vulker against an ongoing Necron invasion from the Sautekh Dynasty.
Once called Talledus, this shrine world was purged and rebuilt after the Age of Apostasy, now held by Sisters of Battle and Imperial Guard regiments.
A dead world in Segmentum Solar, airless and lifeless, noted in Imperial records without further detail.
An ice world where the Grey Knights Second Brotherhood broke an Ork Waaagh and slew the Warboss Snappaklaw along the banks of the River Gandor.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, its records thin and its history largely unwritten.
A remote holding in Segmentum Ultima, known to the archive by name alone.
A stretch of space claimed by the Sons of Jormungandr, a Genestealer Cult whose brood has infested this nebula's shipping and stations.
One of the Black Templars' many roaming Crusade fleets, sailing under a Castellan or Marshal until its sworn objective is fulfilled.
Another Black Templars Crusade fleet, one of the countless independent warbands that campaign for years or decades under their own Marshal.
One of the many roaming crusade fleets fielded by the Black Templars, who forgo a fixed Chapter home in favor of ceaseless war across the stars.
A hive world in Segmentum Ultima, its underhive infiltrated by a genestealer cult the Administratum has yet to root out.
An unclassified holding in Segmentum Obscurus, its dossier limited to a name and a position on the chart.
Fought over during the Horus Heresy, when Space Wolves reinforcements drove off a Night Lords assault; millennia later a splinter of Hive Fleet Leviathan consumed it entirely.
An Adeptus Astartes homeworld within the Imperium Sanctus, sheltering the Crimson Raptors Chapter.
A volcanic world near the galactic core that trained the World Eaters Legion before the Heresy, razed by Iron Hands loyalists who brought its burning moon down upon it.
An Imperial world of the Helican Subsector in Scarus Sector, remembered chiefly as the birthplace of the interrogator Alizebeth Bequin.
A mining world that endured brutal purges under High Lord Goge Vandire and later gave rise to a grief-worshipping cult during the Word Bearers' invasion of its system.
Once an unremarkable Imperial frontier world known as Bhorc Prime, it fell to the Orks and became the seat of Warboss Klawjaw's empire along the Ghoul Stars.
A T'au sept prized as the empire's center of learning, home to its finest universities and military academies and to an unusually large Earth Caste population.
A Black Templars crusade fleet currently campaigning through the Nephilim Sector.
An Imperial hive world whose overcrowded stacks funnel countless recruits each year into the ranks of the Astra Militarum.
A world of the Gloomtide Cluster where Space Wolves packs broke a Snakebite Ork siege and restored its water supply, an action later called the Cleansing of Brakhutos.
An Imperial world of Segmentum Obscurus, noted mainly for a landing made there by the renegade Chaos scientist Fabius Bile.
A dead world in Segmentum Ultima, its history reduced to a coordinate on the chart.
An Imperial recruiting ground in Segmentum Obscurus, remembered chiefly for the grinding siege of Haven's Spire in 935.M41, finally broken when Cadian shock troops disabled its void shields.
A necron tomb world in Segmentum Ultima, its sleeping dynasty noted in Imperial surveys but never fully catalogued.
An industrial home of the Iron Knights Chapter in Segmentum Obscurus, its factories ringed with fortifications and its workers driven hard to keep the Chapter armed for war.
A storm wracked jungle death world of the Koronus Expanse, stripped of any lasting structures and prized by beast hunters for the lethal predators drawn from its rainforests.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Obscurus, present in Imperial registries with no further detail preserved in the archive.
A Black Templars crusade fleet under Marshal Mauger, known for brutal discipline toward the worlds it is sent to save and for recent campaigns through the Chalnath Expanse.
A dead world of Segmentum Tempestus, listed in Imperial census records with little else preserved of its history.
An ork world once ruled by the Arch-Maniac, a Deathskulls warlord who fused himself into a captured forge world's engines and raided at will until Ghazghkull folded his empire into the Great Waaagh.
A dead world of Segmentum Pacificus, empty of atmosphere and life, recorded in the archive by little more than its designation.
A hive world of the Calixis Sector, seat of the Josian Reach's Ecclesiarchal authority and site of bitter Legiones Astartes fighting during the Horus Heresy, remembered as the Death of Canopus.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Tempestus, distinct from its more storied namesake, recorded in the archive without further detail.
An industrial world of Segmentum Ultima that also feeds regiments into the Astra Militarum, its forges and its recruiting yards running side by side.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima left reeling after a tyranid splinter fleet struck it in 981.M41, chaos that ork raiders exploited to make off with the Warlord Titan Fury of Cassiopia.
A former Imperial world in Segmentum Ultima seized during the T'au Fifth Sphere Expansion, later the site of an insurgency and a quiet T'au withdrawal that returned it to independence.
A barren world near the forge world of Ryza in Segmentum Ultima, exposed as a hidden necron tomb during a three way clash of White Scars, orks, and the Nihilakh dynasty in 925.M41.
A necron tomb world in Segmentum Ultima, held for a time by eldar exodites during the Great Sleep before Anrakyr the Traveller reclaimed it, its world spirit later passing into Trazyn's collection.
A ruin choked labyrinth world in Segmentum Tempestus where Inquisitor Kryptman once harvested genestealers for his scheme against the orks of Octarius, later overrun by Hive Fleet Leviathan.
A frontier world in Segmentum Obscurus, sparsely settled and lightly governed, noted in Imperial records with little further detail.
A Deathwatch watch fortress in northern Segmentum Ultima, its Astartes keeping vigil over dormant necron dynasties and hunting the genestealer cult known as the Bringers of Enraptured Joy.
The infamous jungle death world of Segmentum Ultima, whose lethal wildlife forges the toughness of the Catachan Jungle Fighters the Astra Militarum draws from its population.
A webway gate charted somewhere in Segmentum Ultima. Its origin and destination remain absent from Imperial records.
A webway gate bearing the name of Cegorach, the Laughing God worshipped by the Harlequins, opening somewhere within Segmentum Obscurus.
A warp storm that has swollen enormously since the Emperor's confinement on the Golden Throne, drowning dozens of nearby systems in its corrupting tides.
A hot, arid recruiting ground for the Black Templars, whose Chapter keep has stood in its northern mountains for four millennia since the world was wrested from the Alpha Legion.
An Imperial colony of the Coronid Deeps, holding to ordinary civilized life amid a region better known for its darker neighbors.
An Imperial mining world of the Coronid Deeps, struck by the renegade Forge Worlds Cyclothrathe and Draykavac during the Horus Heresy.
A slum world whose destitute masses turned to the Genestealer Cult of the Pauper Princes in hopes of escape, letting the cult seize total control before slipping away aboard a stolen supply barge.
Seat of the Ork Empire of Charadon, one of the largest and longest lived Ork domains, whose Arch Arsonist once hurled his hordes against the Crimson Fists' home of Rynn's World.
An asteroid drifting a strange figure eight path around twin stars, its violent gravity forging rare metals worked by penal laborers at the mining post of Chinchare Minehead.
A prosperous agri world of forests and vast lakes, its small population tending crops and fauna that yield rejuvenating elixirs, fine pelts, and rare narcotics.
The first Knight World reclaimed in the Great Crusade, home to the armored lords of House Krast until Horus Heresy virus bombs scoured its jungles into toxic ruin.
An Ork held world in Segmentum Obscurus, cleared by Imperial Guard forces under Mordian General Eisen late in the 41st millennium.
A world of the Imperium Sanctus lying near the Great Rift, its population wrestling with a Genestealer Cult infestation in Segmentum Ultima.
Once a Necron tomb world, later resettled by Imperial colonists, until the Daemon Prince Beublghor butchered its people and left the planet a barren husk.
A world of the Sabbat Worlds Cluster fought over so bitterly during the Crusade that Guardsmen named it the world that refused to die, costing millions of lives across more than a decade of war.
A crystal moon veiled in mildly psychoactive white sands, site of a savage clash between Eldrad Ulthran's Harlequins and the Deathwatch before the moon was destroyed mid ritual.
An agri world of Segmentum Pacificus whose people threw off the rule of a corrupt cardinal during the Plague of Unbelief, rallied by the preacher Dolan Chirosius.
A star of the Koronus Expanse's Unbeholden Reaches, known for unexplained green flares and for the ravings of a dying astropath who spoke of worms beneath a green eyed sun.
A Segmentum Solar world crowned with towering statues of the Emperor and his Primarchs, later defaced in the War of Statues until a Chaos ritual made the toppled giants rise and crush a Titan Legion.
An installation noted on necron dynastic charts of Segmentum Pacificus. Its purpose against the warp goes otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A Necron installation cataloged in Segmentum Solar as a Contra Empyric Nexus. Little else survives in the archive.

Called by the Necrons a Contra Empyric Nexus, this is Imperial shorthand for the Pariah Nexus, a colossal pylon array raised by the Nihilakh Dynasty that blankets the Nephilim Sector in a field smothering the warp.
A feudal world beyond the Imperium's charted borders, home to the Knights of the Raven Chapter, whose Astartes are regarded as myth by the people below.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima, its industry and standing among the Adeptus Mechanicus otherwise unrecorded in the archive.
Once an agricultural world, Cornucopia harbored a Genestealer Cult called the Starchosen until Ultramarines Chapter Master Marneus Calgar ordered its Exterminatus.
A heavily garrisoned hive world that anchored the Agathean Domain, raising the Solar Auxilia cohorts known as the Lord Marshal's Own and remaining loyal after a bloody coup during the Horus Heresy.
Granted to the Fire Hawks Chapter after the Great Malagantine Purge, this world was left uninhabitable before the fifth century of M40, forcing the Chapter into a fleet based existence.
A hive world of Segmentum Obscurus, entered in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A Knight world fallen to the Ruinous Powers near the Siren's Storm, ruled from its Dread Keeps by Chaos Knights amid warring cultists and corrupted nobles.
A hold world of the Leagues of Votann's Greater Thurian League, prized among the Kin as a major trade hub and famed for its formidable defences.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima, noted in Imperial navigational logs but otherwise unrecorded.
A death world unlike most of its kind: the entire planetary body is believed to be a single living organism, rather than merely home to lethal flora and fauna.
Formerly known as Fief 771/38/qz, this civilised world in the Pale Stars was held by secessionists during the Badab War until the loyalist Minotaurs retook it by force.
A hive world scarred by the Cruach Mhorn Massacres, when a Shadow Hawks culling campaign against rebellious factions provoked an uprising by a Chaos cult calling itself the Silent Legions.
A webway gate charted within the Aeldari's ancient network, its passages recorded by name in Segmentum Ultima but otherwise undocumented.
A daemonic forge world of the Dark Mechanicum said to predate the Imperium itself, founded in the Age of Strife and never reached by the Great Crusade.
Capital of the Cabulis System in Segmentum Pacificus, this war world stockpiles munitions to meet its Imperial tithes and has weathered assault from the Ork Waaagh of Warlord Gragnatz.
A webway gate within the Aeldari's labyrinthine network, charted in Segmentum Ultima but with no further record in the archive.
A daemon world adrift in Segmentum Obscurus, its horrors known to the Imperium in name alone.
A dead world in Segmentum Ultima, airless and sterile, noted in Imperial records without further detail.
An uncatalogued world of Segmentum Tempestus, its history absent from surviving archive records.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, noted in the census with no further archive record.
A fallen forge world of the Dark Mechanicus in Segmentum Ultima, given over to daemon forged engines and forbidden technology after embracing Horus during the Heresy.
A hold world of the Leagues of Votann in Segmentum Ultima, tied to the Ymyr Conglomerate and valued for the singular machinery its forges turn out.

The forge world capital of Segmentum Obscurus, seat of a Segmentum Fortress under Administratum command and known for building the Overlord class battlecruiser, though its system now harbors a genestealer infestation.
An Imperial world of Segmentum Obscurus that raises Guard regiments for the Astra Militarum, once saved from an Ork Waaagh by an army group that included the Cadian 423rd Armoured Regiment.
A sparsely settled outpost on the Eastern Fringe seized by the T'au Empire from the Imperium during its third sphere of expansion, after Catachan defenders broke at the Herzen Ridge.
An Ork held battlefield world of the Octarius sector, named by the greenskins for the sheer number of armies it has swallowed in endless war against the Tyranids.
A charted warp storm within Segmentum Tempestus, its cause and effects otherwise unrecorded in the archive.
An Eldar exodite world caught in the fighting over the Nachmund Sector, raided by Black Legion forces under a Warpsmith and defended by Saim Hann craftworld warriors.
Home to the ancient Knight House Vyronii in Segmentum Obscurus, a swamp and forest world whose knights once held off the xenos Mitu and stayed loyal to the Emperor through the Horus Heresy.
A frontier world visited by Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn, where an excavation guarded by a renegade Space Marine uncovered saruthi artifacts among an enslaved population.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, present in the catalog but otherwise undocumented.
A little recorded site within Segmentum Ultima, its name surviving the archive with almost nothing else attached.
A lush but hazardous Knight world of the Cyclops Cluster, held by House Orhlacc until the household vanished without trace after Dark Haven fell silent during the Horus Heresy.
A recruiting ground for the Adeptus Astartes in Segmentum Pacificus, supplying new aspirants to bolster a Chapter's ranks.
A besieged desert world where T'au forces downed an Astra Militarum general's Valkyrie, drawing a Kasrkin squad into the burning sands to hunt him and uncover what lies beneath them.
A necron tomb world of Segmentum Ultima, its dynasty and state of awakening otherwise unrecorded.
Homeworld of House Vextrix, discovered during the Great Crusade and loyal until the Horus Heresy, when its knights turned traitor and its factories passed to the Dark Mechanicus.
An Imperial Knight world whose households are morbid nihilists that welcome death in battle, all but annihilated fending off a splinter of Hive Fleet Hydra after the Great Rift opened.

An Administratum hub system that fell prey to a daemonic cipher fraud aboard the drifting hulk Pilgrim, spreading a plague of madness through its ruling lords until Grey Knights from Titan were called to intervene.

Once a self governed world whose lax planetary governor let discipline collapse into corruption and vanished investigators, Delta Tao lost contact under crushing warp storms and was declared lost, later found overrun by a Tyranid incursion.
A hold of the Leagues of Votann built around the Ghulo industrial works, one of many Kin settlements sustaining the League's mining and manufacturing network at the galactic core.
A Kin hold at the galactic core, prized for sitting astride a major Votann trade route and for the mineral wealth pulled from its mining centres.
One of three agricultural worlds feeding the forge world of Agripinaa, Dentor suffered mysterious raids that slaughtered much of its population and left the sector fearing worse to come.
A triad hive world of the Askellon Sector near the Eye of Terror, its three cities crammed with tens of billions and long known for glass ground into lenses for the sector's lasweapons.
A mining world whose Coreworks turned into a battleground when a Word Bearers and Alpha Legion warband arrived expecting cultists and instead found a genestealer brood already dug into the populace.

Homeworld of the traitor Knight House Mandrakor at the heart of the Nachmund Gauntlet, a cold and razor ridged world armed by an allied dark forge moon and fought over when the Imperium tried and failed to retake it.
A star fortress standing watch over the northern approach to the Nachmund Gauntlet, holding one of the few stable passages through the Great Rift.
A cardinal world remembered as the birthplace of Sebastian Thor, first to brand the tyrant Goge Vandire a heretic, and later the birthplace of the Confederation of Light.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, present in the census with no further record surviving in the archive.
Little is recorded of this unclassified world beyond its place among the charted systems of Segmentum Ultima.

A feral world in the Koronus Expanse's Cinerus Maleficum region, known chiefly for data vaults uncovered in a space hulk drifting through its orbit, a find that lured a wave of doomed expeditions deeper into the Expanse.
Imperial records keep only sparse notes on this unclassified world of Segmentum Solar.
The only habitable world among nineteen gas giants, Dominica Minor grew wealthy as a refinery and shipyard hub before Mortarion's Death Guard seized it during the Battle of the Coronid Deeps.
A Deathwatch watch fortress on the border of Segmentum Solar and Tempestus, its Kill Teams holding the Pankallis Sub sector against ork and tyranid incursions.

Capital world of the Dovar System, sitting near the precious metal rich Gilded Worlds and judged significant enough that the Imperium was relieved when Tau forces turned away from it during the fighting over Prefectia.
Chapter home of the Star Dragons, whose warriors cut ritual scars into their own flesh and inlay them with circuitry that glows like caged starlight.

A volcanic Knight world where human settlers tamed Terran horses and hunted native megasaurs, drawing the ire of native drakes until the riders forged their first Knight suits and turned the tide, founding House Griffith.
An industrial world of Segmentum Ultima whose factories and foundries feed the Imperial Guard's endless need for fresh recruits and war materiel.

Named for the Chaos Lord Drathoni, whose Heldrake breeding grounds are known among renegade Warpsmiths of Segmentum Ultima and who keeps a long alliance with the Slaaneshi warband called the Hedonistarii.
A Necron tomb world claimed by the Novokh Dynasty, lying dormant in Segmentum Ultima until its sleeping legions are roused.
A daemon world adrift in Segmentum Ultima, its horrors left unrecorded beyond the bare classification.
A feral world in Segmentum Ultima whose tribal warriors are regularly tithed to the Imperial Guard as recruits.

Home to a sprawling Ork empire near the Eye of Terror, ruled by the Great Despot Gazgrim and long at war with the rival empire of Gathrog, its warriors fought under Ghazghkull Thraka in the Third War for Armageddon.
A world on the border of Segmentum Tempestus and Ultima where Tallarn regiments broke a Necron incursion in 997.M41.
A civilized world of Segmentum Ultima that has sent recruits into the Imperial Guard alongside its ordinary tithes.
An uncatalogued world charted in Segmentum Solar, its record in the archive limited to bare coordinates.
An isolated world in Segmentum Ultima where Traitor and Loyalist forces clashed in 010.M31 over rumors of a forbidden empyreal engine, an atrocity that halved the population overnight.
A fortress world locked in permanent night by the warp storm Baphamet, overrun by xenos raiders in the wake of the Great Rift's opening.
A cavern mining world seized by the Ork warlord Killzkar, retaken after a year of guerrilla resistance led by Colonel Straken and the Catachan II regiment.
A dead world on the Eastern Fringe of Segmentum Ultima, stripped of all life much like its neighbors Naogeddon, Holda, and Doton.
A world holding Listening Station 272 and the Island of Misquol, once garrisoned by Chaos Titans until the daemonhost Cherubael destroyed one for Inquisitor Eisenhorn.
A volcanic Knight world rich in adamantium ore, guarded for centuries by the rival Households Navaros and Borgius despite their long feud.
A T'au sept world bathed in perpetual twilight by its tidal lock and twin moons, settled during the First Sphere of Expansion after Fire Warriors drove off native reptilian predators.
A cluster in Segmentum Ultima where Chapter Master Jorus Shadowmaw of the White Panthers died putting down a rebellion in 998.M41.
A hive world in the Chromyd System whose garrisons fell during the Death Guard's Charadon campaign, when Chaos Knights of the Company Malevolent broke its hives and preyed on supply convoys.
A warp storm troubling Segmentum Obscurus, noted in Imperial charts but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A world where the crime lord Phant Mastik once auctioned a rogue Alpha Plus psyker, and where Inquisitor Eisenhorn uncovered the radical Lyko in league with the daemonhost Cherubael.
An uncatalogued world of Segmentum Ultima, its details absent from the archive beyond its recorded existence.
Once held by the Necrons, this dead world in Segmentum Ultima was scoured by exterminatus in 998.M41, burned clean by three companies of the Sable Swords.
Once a shrine world of the Imperial cult, Eldwyn's Lament has since fallen under the Ruinous Powers and is named among the Chaos held worlds of the Despoiler's forces.

An Imperial world in Segmentum Solar that once guarded the Cadian Gate, sacked when Abaddon's Fourth Black Crusade broke the fortress of Kromarch. It was later rebuilt as a memorial to the Cadian dead.
Elsy'eir is a T'au sept world raised from Commander Puretide's victories in the Second Sphere of Expansion, its many moons mined for the ores that feed the battlesuit forges.
An artificial world in Segmentum Ultima, home to the reclusive Doom Legion Chapter, who garrison the ancient star fortress Faithful's Deliverance and watch the Elusian Maze.
A verdant civilised world of Segmentum Solar, some thirty light years from Armageddon, whose Guard tithe raises the Elysian Drop Troops, elite grav chute airborne regiments.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Pacificus, noted in Imperial star charts but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A battle fortress in Segmentum Ultima, reactivated by unknown forces in 997.M41. Valhallan Guardsmen under Commander Kubrik Chenkov drew enemy fire so demolition crews could breach its walls.
A starfort in Segmentum Obscurus whose machine spirit once repelled a daemonic virus assault with the aid of Imperial Fists Techmarine Hestion, until Captain Darnath Lysander broke the siege.
An Imperial Navy base in Segmentum Ultima, logged in fleet records but otherwise absent from the wider archive.
A frontier world of Segmentum Ultima within the Endymion Cluster, settled late and loosely governed, alongside neighbors such as the feudal world Iblis III.
A fortress world in Segmentum Obscurus, marked as one of the exclusion posts raised beyond Imperial space after the Rangdan Xenocides to bar further expeditions past its borders.
A feral world of dense, lightless forests in Segmentum Obscurus. Its tribal population wages endless war while a permanent Imperial blockade fleet forbids any landing on the surface.
The Ennua system in Segmentum Ultima became a flashpoint of the Chalnath Expanse campaign, its industry seized by the T'au before Imperial reinforcements moved to retake it.
Erenon, in Segmentum Ultima, is the homeworld of the Celestial Guard Chapter, whose companies were recalled from every front late in the 41st Millennium to defend it from an ork invasion.
Esko lies in the Reductus Sector of Segmentum Tempestus and is recorded as the homeworld of the Dark Hunters Chapter.
A cardinal world of Ultramar in Segmentum Ultima, once an agri world feeding the Eastern Marches before its forests hid a growing tyranid nest and, later, drew a Plague Marine assault in 109.M42.
An industrial world of Segmentum Obscurus standing at the southern approach to the Nachmund Gauntlet, its factories given wholly over to Imperial manufacture.
A forge world of Segmentum Solar and home to Legio Tempestor, Estaban supplied void craft and war engines during the Horus Heresy and was saved from a later Chaos incursion by the Iron Hands.
Within the Estaban System lies a titan forge world of Segmentum Ultima, home to Legio Magna and known for turning out Baneblade tanks alongside its war engines.

A barren, airless world holding an Imperial reclusiam built around a dormant webway gate, guarded in secret by the Blood Ravens. Thousand Sons sorcerer Mordant Hex overran it, opening the shrine to vacuum and seizing the gate.
A stretch of space where the barrier between the warp and reality runs thin, its currents scattering the planets of the Khan Group across the surrounding void.
An Imperial Navy base in Segmentum Solar, holding the line for the battlefleets that patrol the Imperium's core.
An ork held world in the north eastern reaches of Segmentum Pacificus, where the Cadian 122nd Armoured Regiment led the counterattack against Waaagh Bigtoof in 997.M41.
A world given over to Khorne after a raider called the Red Messiah turned its astropathic choir toward the Maelstrom, drawing Red Corsairs and Berzerker warbands down upon it.
A shrine world walled around the holy city of Eydol, honored as the birthplace of Saint Konaldine. It fell to the returning Word Bearers of the fallen Kor Megron during the Battle of Eagle Gate.
A Deathwatch stronghold in Segmentum Ultima, one of the watch fortresses that keep vigil over the Imperium's borders.
An Astartes world within Segmentum Solar, its name recorded among the Chapter homeworlds but little more surviving in the archive.
A star fort held by the Adeptus Custodes on the star line between Sol and Gathalamor, standing watch over the approaches to the Imperial homeworld.
A world whose people live within honeycombed cave systems, raising the Faeburn Vanquishers, an Astra Militarum regiment drilled and uniformed after the Cadian model.
A death world in Segmentum Ultima where a genestealer infestation has taken root, recorded in the census but little else survives in the archive.

A T'au sept world founded in the first wave of expansion, its Earth Caste prized for innovation and its factories supplying munitions across the empire. Commander O'Shi'ur died defending it from a tyranid invasion.

Once an Imperial world worked under the heavy hand of the Adeptus Mechanicus, until a genestealer smuggled aboard the freighter Redspark seeded the cult now known as the Bladed Cog, which has since seized the planet whole.
The homeworld of the White Panthers Space Marine Chapter.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, recorded in the census but otherwise undocumented.
A grimy hive world of the Calixis Sector, its Hive Magnagorsk forging warship armor for Battlefleet Calixis and its Library of Knowing kept as a private holding of House Vaahkon.
A world of ferocious beasts that supplies the Black Templars with recruits, home to one of their Chapter houses.
The first T'au sept established by the Fifth Sphere of Expansion, seat of a Water Caste command overseeing newly absorbed peoples and home to shipyards across its three moons.
A dead world circling a neutron star in the Belt of Iron, seized by traitor forces as a staging ground for the invasion of Atar-Median during the Cataclysm of Iron.
The homeworld of the Aurora Space Marine Chapter, its surface given over to industrial complexes on a scale rivaling forge worlds, where the Chapter's vehicles are built.
A T'au Sept world in Ultima Segmentum, seized from an Ork warlord during the Third Phase of Expansion at heavy cost. Its settlers are remembered for refusing to yield despite that price.
An agri world of the Mawdlin System, its Grox and Bovian herds feed neighboring worlds. For years the population answered to a Genestealer cult hidden beneath the surface.
An agri world of the Scarus Sector, where drovers march vast herds of migratory beasts across the plains to seasonal trading moots for offworld sale.

An Imperial world whose regiments served the Ordo Xenos against Genestealer incursions elsewhere. Its name carries a lasting stain after dozens of its soldiers defected to the T'au during one campaign.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima. The Imperium records little beyond its name and rough position on the star charts.

Once an Imperial world in Segmentum Obscurus, it fell to Chaos and became the throne of the daemon prince Mk'lrathirix. Schaeffer's penal legion hunted the daemon down there late in the 41st Millennium, at ruinous cost.
A lawless void port anchoring the Koronus Expanse, linking Port Wander to the wider Koronus Passage. Renegades, xenos traders and pirates mix freely under its uneasy truce.

A fortress world the Iron Warriors held under Warsmith Kolvax, ringed by bastions, minefields and void shields. Hive Fleet Leviathan broke it from below, tunnelling Trygons beneath its trenches in 997.M41.
A desolate world in Segmentum Solar, recorded chiefly as the place where the Grey Knights ran the heretic Inquisitor Vetrix to ground in 832.M33.

A hive world of the Sabbat Worlds Cluster that held against Chaos occupation. Its hive city labyrinths cost the Imperial Guard dearly until White Scars Space Marines broke the siege at Operation Redrake.
A Deathwatch fortress standing watch over Segmentum Ultima, part of the Chapter's network of archives, garrisons and training halls for its kill teams.
A Deathwatch fortress in Segmentum Solar, currently commanded by Watch Master Astoren Korr. Its kill teams have been sent as far as the Tyranid ravaged Irontower System.
A fortress world in Segmentum Ultima. Its garrison and defences are recorded only in passing by the wider archive.
An Adeptus Astartes world in Segmentum Ultima. Beyond its role sustaining a Chapter's forces, little further detail survives in the archive.
A warp storm marked in Segmentum Ultima, known to the Imperium by name alone.
A mobile Deathwatch fortress in Segmentum Ultima, driven by plasma engines that make up a third of its mass. Its kill teams watch over the Ork and Tyranid war consuming the Octarius Sector.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima, noted on Imperial star maps with little further record.

An Imperial forest world in Segmentum Ultima built around a major promethium refinery. Space Wolves companies fought through its polluted woodland to retake that refinery from Orks during Ghazghkull's great Waaagh.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Obscurus, present in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.

A world where the Thousand Sons lured Great Wolf Harek Ironhelm and the bulk of his fleet into a decoy battle, drawing the Space Wolves away from Fenris while Magnus the Red struck at their homeworld.
A forge world of the Imperium, granted by Roboute Guilliman to house the Titan Legion Legio Praesagius after the Horus Heresy.
A daemon world claimed by the Ruinous Powers, noted chiefly in catalogues of the galaxy's warp touched territories rather than for any single defining event.
Home to a notorious penal colony whose convicts were pressed into Colonel Schaeffer's infamous Last Chancers regiment.
A feral world under a toxic, corrosive sky that killed the unprotected within moments, fought over by Raptors, Astral Claws and Mantis Warriors during the Badab War.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima with no further record in the archive.
A fortress world in the Yasan Sector overrun by Huron Blackheart's Red Corsairs, later used to stage raids on the White Scars' homeworld of Chogoris.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, otherwise absent from the archive.
A hive world of unspoiled plains and volcanic mountains whose equatorial mines yield crystal stock for the Imperium's phase systems, its main hive Kravaster once besieged by an Ork Waaagh.
A world that sided with the Warmaster during the Horus Heresy, left a lifeless ruin after the Iron Hands salted its five great cities with atomic charges.
Crownworld of the Necron Nihilakh Dynasty, its vaults said to guard the preserved skull of an ancient reptilian oracle prized above all the dynasty's other treasures.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, undocumented beyond its listing in the census.
A destroyed world in Segmentum Ultima, recorded only by its fate and absent any further detail in the archive.
A human settled world where hidden Aeldari pathfinders were rooted out by Imperial Guard forces in a coordinated assault later remembered as the Ghodri Falsehood.
A Necron crown world in Segmentum Ultima, its dynasty and ruling court unrecorded in the surviving archive.
A daemon world charted on Imperial star maps of the region, its particulars otherwise lost to the archive.
Homeworld of the Emperor's Swords Chapter, brought down after a three century Alpha Legion infiltration culminated in a psychic strike that turned corrupted Battle Brothers on their own.

A once compliant mining world whose Trysst cartel was quietly consumed by the Cult of the Four Armed Emperor after a genestealer arrived aboard a fragment of a space hulk, marking the Imperium's first confirmed brush with such a cult.
A warp storm shrouding a remote sub sector known as the Carrion Garden, a blinding haze navigators call the Grey Curtain that few ships manage to cross.
A Necron tomb world of the Sautekh Dynasty granted to the Nemesor Zahndrekh, who expanded his holdings there into a small empire of a dozen star systems.
A contested war world where the Silver Skulls broke Huron Blackheart's Red Corsairs fleet and hunted down the survivors scattered across the Rift's inhabited worlds.

Capital world of the Gilead System, a hive world of nine cities set amid irradiated flats worn bare by millennia of industry. Sixteen billion people endure a world its own resources can no longer support.
A remote stretch of space where the Cult of the Star Kindred has taken root, its followers professing devotion to a sun they call the Emperor while serving something far older beyond the stars.

An Imperial Knight world and seat of House Thale, a fervently devout household that emptied the planet of its Knights to ride out on the witch hunting Malefica Crusade after the Great Rift split the galaxy.
A contested world of the Sabbat Worlds Cluster, held by the Blood Pact until Volpone Bluebloods and allied Guard regiments fought to retake its Agria island chain and the city of Lodden.
A warp wracked site within the Eye of Terror, remembered chiefly as the battlefield where the Space Wolf Njal Stormcaller earned his name, calling down a psychic tempest that destroyed a Chaos host.
A recruiting world of the Adeptus Astartes in Segmentum Tempestus, home to the Firebreakers Chapter of Space Marines.

Also called Angelis, a world of the Ghost Stars region where a crashing Ork space hulk gouged out the canyon named Da Skid. Orks, mutated humans, tribal Diggas and Necrons now share its broken ground.

A forge world of the Gothic Sector, once a hive world surveyed during the Great Crusade before its output passed wholly to the Adeptus Mechanicus. Both the sector and the war fought there take their name from it.

A world besieged by the Ork Bad Moons Clan, whose fall would have starved countless dependent worlds. Space Wolves, Imperial Guard and Sisters of Battle broke the siege to keep its harvests in Imperial hands.

Called the Crown of Miracles, the most prosperous forge world of the Belt of Iron, its flying manufactories capable of building Titans up to Warlord class. Late in the 41st millennium an Ork horde under Warboss Grimskull tried to seize its arsenal.

A dead world whose rogue star is slowly carrying it out of the galaxy, its surface ruined since the Age of Strife. Treasure hunters still risk its xenos and Psychneuein for the Dark Age relics buried there.
An industrial and fortress world that was a hidden fiefdom of the Dark Angels Legion during the Great Crusade and Horus Heresy, its vaults holding proscribed weapons kept from the Mechanicum's sight.
A Dark Angels stronghold of industry from the Great Crusade era, its sealed armories once stocked with forbidden war engines the Legion kept secret from the rest of the Imperium.
A webway gate recorded in Segmentum Tempestus, its history otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A hidden sanctum reached through the webway, ranked among the aeldari's most sacred places and bound to the worship of Asuryan, greatest of their gods.
A hold world of the Greater Thurian League in the Leagues of Votann, set near the galactic core along a trade route beside three major Thurian mining sites.

A minor agri world in the Damocles Gulf, almost ringed by Tau territory, where a Tau trade mission and a hidden Genestealer Cult both took root before Imperial Guard regiments and Inquisitor Amberley Vail intervened.
A world recorded in the Segmentum Ultima census, its classification unresolved and its history otherwise unrecorded in the archive.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Solar, noted in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.

An isolated world of the Eastern Fringe's Ysobel Cloud, its population annihilated by the Night Lords in 843.M34 through weeks of psychological terror that left the planet in permanent radioactive night.
A world of the Sabbat Worlds, taken by the Imperium during the Cabal Salient offensive of the Sabbat Worlds Crusade.
A daemon world bound to the Ruinous Powers within Segmentum Tempestus, its record otherwise bare.
A dead world of Segmentum Obscurus, its atmosphere and life long since stripped away, little else noted in the archive.
Once a mighty forge world of the Alduin Sector, remembered as the Lost Forge after Hive Fleet Leviathan overran it in 997.M41; a handful of tech priests escaped with its blueprints.
A forge world on the fringe of Segmentum Ultima that served as a loyalist command base during the Horus Heresy and the Great Scouring, refitting warships for the campaigns that followed.
A dead world in Segmentum Obscurus, catalogued but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
An ork stronghold in Segmentum Ultima ruled by the Big Mek Gurgit, who named it for himself and launched a walker heavy Waaagh against the T'au from its surface.
A mining world in Segmentum Pacificus whose miners rose up and drove out the forces of Cardinal Bucharis during the Plague of Unbelief, the second world to do so after Chiros.
A warp storm at the heart of the Jericho Reach in Segmentum Ultima, known to the Imperium as the red hole; several daemon worlds are said to lie within its bounds.

An Imperial mining world in the Chalnath Expanse, cut off beyond the Great Rift and besieged by chaos cults, mutants, a genestealer cult and T'au invaders in the fighting known as the Haephos Containment.
A world of Segmentum Ultima recorded in Imperial charts, its history otherwise lost to the archive.
An Eldar exodite world overrun by Hive Fleet Naga in the early ninth century of M41; its guardians made a doomed last stand to protect the world spirit before the swarm consumed it.
A world of Segmentum Ultima tied to Ultramarines and Dark Angels operations during the Horus Heresy, though the archive holds little more than that.
A world of Segmentum Ultima, recorded in the census but otherwise undocumented.
A webway gate charted within Segmentum Ultima, one of the hidden passages threading the Eldar's ancient road between the stars.
A ring of ancient, mostly dying stars circling the galaxy's edge in Segmentum Obscurus, largely unexplored and held by the Imperium to be haunted; expeditions sent into it seldom return.
A Deathwatch watch fortress in Segmentum Obscurus, standing ready to muster kill teams drawn from many Space Marine Chapters.
An Imperial Guard recruiting world of Segmentum Ultima whose regiments fought and were lost during the Fallaxian Scourging, later branded deserters by a Munitorum unaware of their fate.
An Imperial world of Segmentum Solar that has furnished the Astra Militarum with regiments for its wars.
An Imperial world in the wider archive's unlisted reaches, site of a clash between Eldar raiders and the Cadian 423rd Armoured Regiment.
A warp rift that flared to sudden violence near Atilla, staining the night sky with a reddish glow across the Corvus Cluster and threatening to drown out the Astronomican's light.
A Black Templars crusade fleet under Marshal Gheidon, folded into Battle Group Erastus of the Indomitus Crusade's Fleet Secundus and blooded freeing Vastoros from a rogue psyker's rule.
One of several Black Templars crusade fleets keeping the watch over Segmentum Solar, ready to muster alongside its sister crusades whenever a shrine world calls for aid.

An old Cardinal World reclaimed by the Ecclesiarchy after centuries under tyrant rule, its holy road lined by the towering Cairns of Canticus. In 980.M41 Salamanders and Cadian troops broke a Black Legion invasion here.
An unclassified point on the charts of Segmentum Ultima, its nature left unrecorded in the archive.
An uncharted world of Segmentum Obscurus, noted in the census without further detail surviving.
A named warp storm charted among the Imperium's recorded Immaterium hazards in Segmentum Ultima, its shifting boundary a lasting threat to fleets routing nearby.
An unclassified location entered in the registries of Segmentum Ultima, its particulars otherwise absent from the archive.
A Greenskin held world where, in 844.M41, Rogue Trader Maximillian Trusk fought off native Ork tribes to recover archeotech weapons dating to the Dark Age of Technology.

Once chief world of the Aegis Sector and an Agri World tilled by slave labor after the Night Lords broke it during the Great Crusade. Its loyalty to Horus earned it an eighteen week siege by the Ultramarines that left no survivors.
A world of Segmentum Ultima noted only in passing among the Imperium's planetary listings, its history otherwise unrecorded.
Homeworld of Folker, a Black Templars Castellan who left this Segmentum Obscurus world to serve among the Chapter's Chaplains.

A Feral World in the galactic north of Segmentum Ultima near the Halo Stars, its tribes marking every battle won with fresh tattoos. It is homeworld to the Novamarines, whose Fortress Novum crowns its highest peak.

In 453.M40 the public execution of 666 heretics here unwittingly completed a daemonic ritual, tearing a gate into the Warp. The Slaaneshi Prince Laesydra seized the governor's body before the Grey Knights intervened.
A desert Feudal World of the Askellon Sector whose clans dwell among carved canyons and caves, keeping legends of warriors who fell from the sky and wary tales of warp touched Furimancers.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its designation preserved in the census with no further record surviving.

A world that held out for months against the Necron Sautekh Dynasty before Imotekh the Stormlord's Siege of Hypnoth broke it in 911.M41, after a technophagic virus crippled its Adeptus Mechanicus defences.
A system of Segmentum Ultima entered in the Imperium's planetary registries, its particulars otherwise lost to the archive.
Homeworld of the Tigers Argent Chapter, positioned uncomfortably close to both the Great Rift and the Pariah Nexus. Refugees and stranded Imperial forces have gathered there in search of shelter.

A hive and industrial world of Segmentum Ultima, prized for its ore and myco protein exports. Genestealer cults rose there twice, and Marneus Calgar slew the Swarmlord in single combat as the Kraken tyranid fleet closed in.
A necron tomb world in Segmentum Ultima, its sleeping dynasty recorded only in fragmentary Imperial reports.

A lesser known Asuryani craftworld standing close to the Eye of Terror, its Bonesingers turned from artistry to war after the Fall. Its wraith constructs and warhosts wage an unending campaign against the servants of Chaos.

A dead world of the Koronus Expanse, orbiting a fuel starved dying star and scoured by shatterstorms capable of flaying flesh from bone. Explorers found alien crypts there, lined with mummified xenos remains.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Tempestus, its position charted on Imperial star maps but little else recorded of it.
The battle fleet of the Imperial Fists Legion, once counting some 1,500 warships. During the Horus Heresy it fought clear of an Iron Warriors ambush at Phall to answer the summons back to Terra.
A battle group of the Imperial Fists Chapter operating in Segmentum Ultima, its movements and engagements recorded only in scattered fleet logs.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, noted in Imperial cartography but otherwise absent from the archive.

A forge world near Dragon's End, once home to the Legio Fureans before their fall to Chaos. The Iron Warriors besieged it at ruinous cost during the Great Crusade, and it later pledged itself to Horus.
A fortress world known as Incalpeta Terminus, standing at the edge of the charted galaxy during the age of the Horus Heresy. Little more of its garrison or purpose survives in Imperial record.

A death world where a governor's private menagerie of giant predators escaped captivity and now roams unchecked. The Indigan Praefects regiment, hardened by hunting them, is called upon wherever monstrous xenos threaten the Imperium.
A Deathwatch fortress in Segmentum Ultima, its Kill Teams equipped with Hellblasters to counter the tyranid and ork monstrosities at its borders.

Twin singularities locked in a gravitic dance, sheltering Chaos raiders under the Sorcerer Kazik Gyle. The Inquisition has sealed all record of the site, though rebellions traced to it have spread across dozens of worlds.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima, its churning currents cutting off nearby systems from Imperial contact for years at a stretch.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Tempestus, its designation preserved in Imperial gazetteers though little else of it survives in the archive.
Homeworld of the Angels of Fury Space Marine Chapter, its standing within Segmentum Solar rarely touched upon beyond that founding role.

A Chaos held world in the Koronus Expanse's Dioskouri System, its acid seas and shattered moon overlooking mines worked hollow since the dawn of human spaceflight. The warlord Karrad Vall now rules its corrupted hives and shipyards.

A fortress world whose governor once got drunk and bragged that its new bastions made the planet unassailable. Alpha Legion agents passed the boast to the Iron Warriors, who starved the population into submission and seized it as a Chaos stronghold.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, noted only in passing among records tied to the Horus Heresy era. The archive holds little else on it.
A dead world of Segmentum Obscurus, its atmosphere and biosphere long since stripped away. No further chronicle of its fall survives.
The battlefleet of the Iron Crusaders Chapter, an Adeptus Astartes force last recorded pushing deeper into the Ghoul Stars beyond the Imperium's charted reach, with no confirmed word from it since.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, present in the census but otherwise absent from the wider record.
A dead world claimed as a Necron tomb, where the Word Bearers ground through a long war against awakened constructs and only broke the stalemate by calling daemons of Khorne into the fight.

An Aeldari craftworld given over to Morai Heg, crone goddess of the underworld, whose farseers spend lifetimes hunting relics from the lost Crone Worlds. Its warhosts draw unusually heavily on the blade sisterhoods of the Howling Banshees.

Once home to an Ork freebooter kabal called the Star Krumpas, Jagga was stripped bare when the tendrils of Hive Fleet Behemoth passed through, leaving it catalogued among the Imperium's devoured worlds.
Homeworld of the Blood Swords, a Chapter raised from Blood Angels gene seed, who campaign in close partnership with the neighboring Star Dragons of Draconith.
A world of the Jakart Cluster where Lord Solar Macharius himself was gravely wounded fighting Orks, and where the Angels Revenant later earned renown clearing the cluster of its remaining foes.

A Knight World hidden inside a warp storm until Imperial envoys arrived in late M36 and were slaughtered by House Herpetrax. The reprisal invasion collapsed into mass mutation, leaving the house to rule openly as worshippers of Chaos.

One terminus of the warp gate linking the Koronus Passage to the Jericho Reach, found by the frigate Spear of Tarsus in the eighth century of M41 and later used to launch the Achilus Crusade into the Eastern Fringe.

The paired terminus of the same warp gate, a crescent of dark stone standing alone in open space. Matter entering one end reappears near instantly at the other, bypassing years of ordinary warp travel.

A long isolated system in Segmentum Pacificus that had grown prosperous and independent before Imperial rediscovery, and whose scorn for outside rule flared into a rebellion the Black Templars crushed in the Jerulas Crusade.

Once the forge world Dironth, renamed in mockery after annihilating the fleet of the Rogue Trader it was named for. Contact was lost amid the Cataclysm of Iron, and a later envoy found only a silent world whose automated guns still fired on approach.

An ocean world whose polar caps were deliberately melted on the order of High Lord Goge Vandire, drowning billions in the cities of Omon and Vorlencia. Centuries later Marneus Calgar led the campaign that cleared its waters of Chaos infiltrators.
A death world scarred by fields of burning sulphur, harsh enough that it still musters hardened regiments for the Astra Militarum.

An agri world briefly overrun by a vanguard tendril of Hive Fleet Moloch, remembered chiefly for the Standard Template Construct fragments unearthed there, which Guard regiments held the spaceport long enough for the Adeptus Mechanicus to evacuate.
The homeworld of the Honoured Sons Chapter, set among the Eastern Fringes of Segmentum Ultima.
A twilight death world overrun by Hive Fleet Leviathan during the Third Tyrannic War, where Catachan Jungle Fighters held the line until Imperial reinforcements broke the tyranid advance.
A jungle world of Segmentum Pacificus brought under Imperial control during the Macharian Crusade, its conquest requiring several Catachan regiments alongside Mordian troops.
An old colony of the forge world Mezoa, given over to promethium and chemical refineries that burned when Mezoa's rivalry with M'Pandex turned to open war during the Horus Heresy.
Capital world of the Askellon Sector and reputedly the first of its Founding Worlds to be settled, its surface long since reduced to toxic swamp beneath sprawling, mostly abandoned hives.

An Ork held world of Segmentum Tempestus, contested for generations, remembered for Valhallan Captain Kubrik Chenkov holding its defences for three days against a greenskin assault until relief arrived.
An Imperial world noted only briefly in Astra Militarum source material, its history otherwise absent from the archive.
A world of Segmentum Ultima whose record in the archive amounts to little more than its name and coordinates.
Namesake world of the Kaelas system, besieged alongside neighboring Sessec by the Red Corsairs warband in 999.M41.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima that supplies the Adeptus Mechanicus war effort, its deeper history unrecorded in surviving archive fragments.

A forge world littered with pre Age of Strife ruins once thought to rival Mars, dominated for millennia by the fanatical tech cult Endeavour of Airless Purity before the Imperium's arrival, and later a traitor stronghold during the Cataclysm of Iron.
A hive world of the Prismata System in Segmentum Obscurus, devastated when Chaos forces overran the Stygius Sector.
A world of the Kalium system, home to the ruined Kalium Gate, a Dark Age warp relic destroyed by Perturabo and later fought over by the White Scars and Emperor's Children.

An industrial world seized during the Macharian Crusade, famed for the day Lord Solar Macharius broke an ork horde twenty three billion strong, a battle orks themselves still recall as one of their greatest scraps.

A volcano wracked feral world whose barbarian tribes have grown closer to Ogryn than baseline human, later revealed to conceal a slumbering Necron tomb when the Word Bearers invaded seeking a way inside.
Once a forge world whose rulers struck an alliance with the servants of Tzeentch, drawing an invasion from the Death Guard's Second Plague Company before both sides turned against the attackers.
A world left desecrated by the experiments of the renegade Chaos Space Marine Fabius Bile.

Capital of the Brezantius System and one of the Metalican Gates guarding the Obolis Sub sector of Charadon, held as a major fortress world through repeated Chaos assaults during the Charadon Campaign.
The Segmentum Fortress of Ultima and a naval academy hosting the shipyards that build Dominator class cruisers, now stranded in the Dark Imperium near the Great Rift.
Capital world of the breakaway Severan Dominate, its densely populated holdings became prime recruiting ground once Duke Severus XIII raised his rebellion against the Imperium.
A small, mineral rich colony settled by the Imperium in the 38th Millennium, later overrun by Waaagh! Garaghak and refashioned into the Ork stronghold of Mekslag Ikks.
A shadow world near Catachan where the Unseen Lurkers regiment fought a grinding, low visibility campaign against Deathskulls Orks and their hidden Stompa factory.
A drifting field of mineral rich rock in Segmentum Ultima where fleeing Ork remnants of Ullanor once dug in, before Blood Angels and Alpha Legion forces hunted them down and scoured the belt clean.
A daemon world of the Ruinous Powers kept under perpetual Imperial blockade in Segmentum Obscurus, its true nature recorded only in passing among the sector's other lost worlds.
A Deathwatch watch fortress in Segmentum Obscurus that came to sit near a cascade of overlapping warp storms after the Great Rift tore open, repelling repeated Traitor assaults at heavy cost.
A world of Segmentum Solar held as the home of the Skull Bearers Space Marine Chapter.
A T'au sept near the Perdus Rift whose people, hardened by centuries of alien incursions, turned constant warfare into innovation, birthing the Y'vahra, R'varna and Tau'nar battlesuits.
An unremarkable system of Segmentum Ultima, catalogued but otherwise absent from the wider archive.
A water locked agri world along a warp route near the Cadian Gate whose kelp harvests fed millions of Imperial citizens, until crushing taxation sparked the Khai Zhan Uprising in 968.M41.
A webway gate of Segmentum Ultima, named for the Eldar war god Kaela Mensha Khaine, its passages recorded but their history largely lost to the archive.
A world of the Khan Group fought over during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade, where Lord General Bulledin's forces broke Chaos Magister Shebol Red Hand across the twin hives of Khena and Khenor.
An Adeptus Astartes world of Segmentum Tempestus, home to a Space Marine Chapter that keeps its fortress monastery there.
A system of Segmentum Obscurus prized for its refineries and shipyards, seized by Tzeentch's hordes during the invasion of the Stygius Sector and never fully retaken.
A system of Segmentum Tempestus hosting a keep of the Black Templars, one of many scattered footholds the Chapter maintains across the Imperium.
A mining world adrift in the Khymaran Drift near the Maelstrom in Segmentum Ultima. During the Badab War its lunar outposts saw the Howling Griffons badly mauled by the Executioners.

An Imperial Knight World cloaked for millennia by the Black Pall, a nebula so thick that House Mortan hunted in near total darkness until the shroud thinned in late M35. Its knights favour close combat above all.
A Necron Tomb World lying near the Halo Stars, one of many such vaults noted in Imperial chronicles of the Awakening Empire but rarely detailed beyond its bare designation.
Once an Imperial shrine world in Segmentum Solar, ruined when the warband of Chaos Lord Revellian Thrice Burned overran it and bent it toward the worship of Slaanesh.

The battle fleet of the Knights of Gryphonne, a Crusading Chapter without a fixed homeworld, last tracked near the Reductus Sector in Segmentum Tempestus. Its Astartes wear ochre plate marked only with a black aquila.
An Imperial world in Segmentum Ultima that rose up in rebellion late in M41, only to have the revolt broken by the Mordian 56th Regiment, who executed a tenth of the populace to keep it loyal.
Recorded chiefly as Konig Prime, a world of Segmentum Ultima whose levy fills the ranks of the Konig Armoured Regiments, fielding companies of Leman Russ tanks and Baneblades for the Imperial Guard.
A onetime shipbuilding world of the Realm of Ultramar, ranked among its four ruling planets. Mortarion's Death Guard tried to break through its system during the Plague Wars but were repelled.

A T'au sept world of the Nem'yar Atoll caught in permanent night as it circles a black hole, kept liveable only by thermo inductive shields. Automated ports there harvest dark matter to fuel Riptide battlesuits.

An Imperial world in Segmentum Ultima remembered for a year long siege, broken at last when Commander Kubrik Chenkov stormed its enemy citadel without artillery support, at a cost of ten million Guardsmen.
A death world of Segmentum Ultima where crystalline creatures roam that no weapon has yet managed to shatter.

A Knight World rediscovered in 112.M41 once the warp storm sealing it away for twenty thousand years finally broke. Its two knightly houses came to blows before Baron Jakobus brokered an uneasy peace.
Krandor III burned under Exterminatus in 853.M41 after Alpha Legion agitators drove its cults into open revolt, a fate its exiled Krandorian Guard regiments swore to avenge.

A Knight World shadowed by black hills and cold rain, sheltered somewhat by its nearness to Baal. House Hawkshroud's nobles shun advanced comforts and once turned back an invasion by the Necron Mephrit Dynasty.

A death world ravaged by a civil war so devastating its survivors spent five centuries clawing the surface back from ruin. Its regiments, the Death Korps, seek redemption through self sacrifice in the Imperium's worst warzones.

A hive world overrun by Warboss Ghazghkull's Waaagh during the Psychic Awakening. Wolf Lord Ragnar Blackmane hunted the ork there and nearly died in the clash, later undergoing the Rubicon Primaris to survive his wounds.
A contested world fought over by Blood Ravens, T'au, Eldar and Necrons alike. Brother Captain Davian Thule led the Chapter's search for ancient relics there from their stronghold at North Vandea.

Ksi'myen was among the first septs founded during the T'au Third Sphere Expansion, folding in worlds once claimed by the Imperium. Its resettled human citizens are noted for a cultural bent toward subtlety and opportunism.

Kyro IV serves the Imperium as a mining world in the Pale Stars region, first brought under Imperial control when the Great Crusade's 771st Expedition Fleet swept through the sector in the thirtieth millennium.
Recorded in the archive as an uncharted holding of Segmentum Ultima, its allegiance and history otherwise lost to the surviving records.
An unclassified world logged within Segmentum Ultima, its affiliation and past absent from the archive's surviving records.
Lanorra is a hive world of the Imperium sited within Segmentum Obscurus, its stacked cities packed into one of the segmentum's harsher reaches.

Larsa II mines ore for the Imperium in the Pale Stars region of Segmentum Ultima, one of several worlds in its system marked by a mutant population and a history of slaughter during the Great Crusade.

Lascal began as an Ork held world of the Coronid Deeps before the Great Crusade's 7th Expeditionary Fleet cleared it and built it into a major naval anchorage. It fell to the Death Guard early in the Horus Heresy, left a poisoned wreck.

Lastrati endured the World Eaters during the Horus Heresy and, centuries later, a eugenic sect called the Divine Army that bred tailored plagues, wiping out all but a fraction of its billions before the Black Templars broke it in 543.M36.

The Lathes are a trio of forge worlds in the Calixis Sector's Golgenna Reach, their shared irregular orbit producing bursts of hyper gravity that Mechanicus smiths prize for forging unusually dense blades and shot.
Latona was a shipyard world of Ultramar, home to the Fulgentius Shipyards and part of the Andarion Fleet's base, until the World Eaters shattered its docks during the Horus Heresy.
Lavantia is a desert hive world of Segmentum Tempestus and homeworld to the Night Swords Space Marines, whose Chaplains walk its dune seas seeking visions of the Chapter's honored dead.
A forbidden world of Segmentum Tempestus, quarantined and struck from the common star charts, the reason for its exclusion unrecorded in what survives of the archive.

Leckides is a rad scarred world remembered for a Space Wolves landing during the war against Ghazghkull's Waaagh!, where bike and cavalry companies broke an Ork speed mob before it could reach the warlord's main horde.
Lentrel Prime is a forge world counted among the eighteen holdings of the Vidar Sector, from which the Imperium lost all contact in 925.M41.

Lethe served the Great Crusade as an early staging world and later became home to the Flame Falcons Chapter, until the Inquisition answered the Chapter's mutation with a Grey Knights purge and Exterminatus, leaving only ash.
An unclassified holding within Segmentum Ultima, noted in the census but otherwise unremarked upon in the surviving archive.
Locale Perdita marks not a world but a gate into the webway, one of the ancient Eldar passages threading beneath realspace toward the scattered remnants of their empire.

The sacred hive world of the Q'orl species, orbited by honeycomb structures built from their own dead. Zealously guarded Q'orl warfleets massacred over thirty thousand stray Imperial pilgrims here in M41.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its name recorded in Imperial cartography but little more preserved in the archive.
A death world of Segmentum Tempestus, its lethal terrain noted in Imperial registries though no further account survives here.

Homeworld of the Fire Angels Chapter and site of their fortress monastery, the Tower Athenaeum. During the Horus Heresy the White Scars fought the Emperor's Children here, a battle the traitors won with Dark Mechanicus support.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima, its output and history otherwise absent from the archive's records.

A memorial world of the Farsight Enclaves, its spires threaded with Tau research tunnels and manufactories. Knights of Blood Space Marines massacred Earth Caste workers here before Commander Farsight's fleet destroyed the attackers.

A hollow forge world in Segmentum Obscurus, its core an artificial sun powering the forges above. Home to the Legio Astorum Titan Legion, the Warp Runners, it produces Warhound Scout Titans and Macharius tanks.
