An Aeldari craftworld known as the Light of the Fallen Suns, active in the western Segmentum Obscurus. Its Asuryani favor corsair tactics and dream of abandoning realspace entirely for a new life within the webway.
A world of Segmentum Ultima lying within the Imperium Nihilus near the Malfactus Warp Storm, named in Death Guard campaign records but otherwise sparsely documented.

A desert death world of Segmentum Tempestus known as Luther Macintyre IX, scoured by sandstorms that can flay flesh from bone. Homeworld of the burrowing Ambull, it supplies the Astra Militarum with Deathworld Veteran regiments.

A minor forge world in the Belt of Iron that turned traitor during the Horus Heresy. In 010.M31 its armies fought the loyalist forges of Graia, Arl'yeth and Atar-Median in the conflict remembered as the Cataclysm of Iron.
Homeworld of the Night Sentinels Space Marine Chapter, lying in the Imperium Nihilus near the Malfactus Warp Storm. The world has faced repeated invasion since the opening of the Great Rift.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, present in Imperial charts but without further surviving record.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Tempestus, catalogued but otherwise unrecorded here.

A shrine world of the Calixis Sector dedicated to Saint Drusus, who fell and rose again upon its soil during the Angevin Crusade. Its Black Order and Maccabian Janissaries regiments hold martial discipline as a form of worship.
Formerly known as Donia, this Segmentum Pacificus shrine world was renamed in honour of Lord Commander Solar Macharius, who launched his crusade here and was later entombed within its marble shrines.

A stretch of space at the heart of the Machorta Sector where, during the Indomitus Crusade, Fleetmaster Cassandra VanLeskus and the Ultramarines broke a Khornate armada attempting to widen the Great Rift into Segmentum Solar.
An industrial world of Segmentum Ultima that suffered raids from Dark Eldar Haemonculi of the Coven of Twelve, who preyed upon its population for their grim experiments.
The site where, during the 7th Black Crusade in 811.M37, Abaddon the Despoiler and the Black Legion all but annihilated the Blood Angels Chapter, leaving their gene-seed ruined beyond recovery.
An agri world of Segmentum Obscurus, feeding Imperial populations elsewhere though little of its own story survives in the archive.
An Imperial world in Segmentum Solar where Space Wolves under Ragnar Blackmane broke a Chaos warband's assault on the Crystal City, only for the fighting to summon a daemonic incursion.
A Daemon World in Segmentum Ultima, given over wholly to the Ruinous Powers. The Imperium keeps no further record of it.
One of Ultramar's principal worlds during the Great Crusade and the Horus Heresy, its fate afterward was never recorded in surviving Imperial archives.
A former agri world in the Jericho Reach that fell under the pull of the Hadex Anomaly, thereafter a daemon-haunted Chaos stronghold used as a breeding ground by the cult known as the Stigmartus.
An Imperial world of Segmentum Obscurus noted chiefly for the Guard regiments it raises for the Imperium.
A Hive World targeted by the Death Guard during the Horus Heresy, where a lone company of Raven Guard held out in a guerrilla campaign for three years before the traitors withdrew.
A warp storm recorded in Segmentum Ultima. Beyond its name, the archive holds nothing further.
An active Imperial war zone in Segmentum Tempestus, sealed under Inquisitorial classification that keeps most details from public record.
A gloomy, hive-choked world of the Calixis Sector, ruled by the Eminence Glydus Matriarch and long regarded by the Ecclesiarchy as fertile ground for heresy and secret cults.
Once home to the Mitu Conglomerate, a psychic xenos cabal that ruled billions of humans in a closed empire until three Space Marine Legions broke it during the Great Crusade.
A war world of Segmentum Tempestus, remembered for the Ebon Sentinels Chapter's fall to Chaos corruption and the Grey Knights sent to purge them.
An Imperial Knight world in Segmentum Ultima, home to House Trainor and to the Howling Griffons, who have deliberately kept its feudal city-states locked in perpetual, primitive warfare.
A Necron crown world on the Eastern Fringe, seat of the Sautekh Dynasty's stasis-crypts and now active again since Phaeron Imotekh roused its legions.
An agri world of Ultramar, its deserts turned fertile by upheaval and later scarred by a Nurgle blight that opened the way for a Death Guard invasion.
A cemetery world infested by the Blessed Wormlings, a Genestealer cult whose acolytes feed on the carrion insects of its graveyards while awaiting the Great Devourer.
A Fallen Knight world ruled by House Khomentis, which turned to Chaos and Dark Mechanicum worship after a daemonic incursion in M33 consumed its deserts.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Obscurus, noted in the census but otherwise absent from the archive.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, recorded only by name in Imperial charts.
A dead world quarantined by the Ordo Xenos as a suspected Necrontyr homeworld, its tomb stirred to war when the Necron Lord called the Herald of the Storm awoke.
A Necron crown world of Segmentum Pacificus, its dynasty and history left undocumented in the archive.
An Imperium world of Segmentum Ultima, noted only in passing census records with no further detail preserved in the archive.
A star system in the Sabbat Worlds region of Segmentum Pacificus, wracked by warp instability and reclaimed from the forces of Chaos during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade.
A volcanic forge world of Segmentum Obscurus raised during the Great Crusade, its hardened lava surface too unstable to bear the weight of Titan war engines.
A world of Segmentum Ultima recorded in Imperial ledgers, its history otherwise absent from surviving archive material.

A forge world of the Charadon Sector, its surface a shell of dull metal with no soil or life, kept under perpetual guard against the nearby Ork empire. Home to the ancient Titan Legion called the Iron Skulls.
A hive world whose population once rose in rebellion, overloading their principal hive's geothermal power grid and destroying it during the unrest known as the Plague of Unbelief.
A hive world that raises the Methuselan regiments of the Astra Militarum, its recruits toughened by conditions before ever taking the oath of the Guard.
A gas giant ringed by orbital stations that harvest its noxious atmosphere, home to the Miasman Redcowls Guard regiments, whose flamer fuel burns an even fouler green than promethium.
A forge world of Segmentum Pacificus that sided with the Word Bearers during the Horus Heresy, later stormed and taken by the Iron Hands.
A civilized world of Segmentum Solar that supplies regiments to the Astra Militarum, its wider history left unrecorded in the archive.
A death world of Segmentum Ultima where the Scythes of the Emperor took refuge after Tyranids consumed their homeworld Sotha, making a final stand at the citadel called the Giant's Coffin.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its record in the archive limited to a name and a segmentum designation.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima and home to the Legio Venator, known as the Iron Spiders, whose foundries turn out Chimeras and Malcador tanks for the Imperium's armies.
A dead world of Segmentum Obscurus, stripped of life and left largely undocumented beyond its classification.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, noted in the census with no further detail surviving.

A Necron tomb world and crown seat of the Nekthyst Dynasty, its interior a shifting maze of hyperspace crypts. Devastated by an Ork assault, it drove its rulers to abandon their old colors for blackened copper.
A dead world of Segmentum Ultima, once a mining colony of the Moloch system stripped of its population within days when Tyranid spores fell upon its surface.

A mining world of the Moloch system that outlasted its sister planets against Tyranid invasion, evacuating most of its people into the fortresses of the Thunderwall mountains before the swarm arrived.
A feudal world of Segmentum Ultima freed from a corrupt planetary governor by the rebel Kyrinov, now inside the region swallowed by the Great Rift and a steady source of Guard regiments.
A hive world whose harsh conditions temper its people before they ever enlist, producing seasoned regiments for the Astra Militarum.
Frozen wilderness world of Segmentum Tempestus, home to the reclusive Red Hunters Chapter and the fortress-monastery from which they hunt down deviants and heretics.
A moon of Segmentum Pacificus near a cluster of Imperial worlds once ravaged by the Slaaneshi raider Lilesh Snarelust, whose horde was finally hunted down and slain by the Eldar of Saim-Hann.
A cluster of moons in Segmentum Obscurus, recorded in Imperial charts but with no further detail preserved in the archive.

A storm-wracked Knight world ruled by House Lucaris from the mountain stronghold of Stratus Keep, its nobles having thrown in with Horus during the Heresy and never returned to Imperial allegiance since.

A sunless mining world of Segmentum Tempestus where clans harvest a corrosive, acid-secreting bacterium through a maze of tunnels, breeding the hardened tunnel-fighters of the Mordant Guard regiments.

A tidally locked hive world of Segmentum Obscurus known as the World of Eternal Night, its billions crowded onto the sunless hemisphere under the rigid rule of the Tetrarchy and the discipline of the Mordian Iron Guard.
Once the Imperial forge world of Mordax, overrun during the Green Kroosade and remade by the Deathskulls klan into a scrapyard of endless gargant construction, its old name lost to Ork slang.
The chapter homeworld of the Dark Eagles in Segmentum Tempestus, seat of their fortress-monastery and recruiting ground for the Chapter's aspirants.

A heavily fortified world of Segmentum Pacificus that anchored Chaos naval power during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade, its long-range batteries threatening reconquered systems until Warmaster Macaroth's forces finally broke it.

A hive world of Segmentum Obscurus, its Guard regiments raised under the same name as the sector capital Morox Excellus, site of a decades-long Horus Heresy battle so brutal it spawned its own war-cults.
An ongoing Black Templars crusade fleet led by Marshal Gorvian Demeer, tasked with clearing a route through the Great Rift near the Galactic Core and now locked in conflict with Votann Kin.

A ruined world of Segmentum Ultima wiped out by the Red Corsairs, remembered chiefly as the place where Grey Knight Vorth Mordrak lost his entire kill-team and became the sole survivor haunted by their memory.
An Imperial Guard recruiting world of Segmentum Tempestus, source of the Mortant regiments including the Mortant 7th, who fought in the sacking of Colonia.
A Deathwatch watch fortress standing in the Imperium Nihilus reaches of Segmentum Ultima, its companies under Watch Master Agathon drawn deep into the war for the Chalnath Expanse.
A storm-battered ocean world of Segmentum Ultima with little dry land beyond volcanic island chains, home to the Excruciators Chapter and held sacred by the peoples of its home system.
A forge world of Segmentum Obscurus feeding war materiel to the Imperium, its records in the archive otherwise sparse.
A volcanic world on the Eastern Fringe of Segmentum Ultima, home to the Fire Lords Chapter, whose flame-rites echo those of the Salamanders without sharing their bloodline.
The drifting husk of the dead craftworld Malan'tai, its hulk still threaded by the webway and reachable through a gate long after the Doom that devoured it moved on.
A hot, arid world of Segmentum Obscurus whose collapsed mega-cities hint at a lost age of high technology, now picked over by tribal herders who no longer understand the ruins around them.

A fortress world of Segmentum Tempestus raised by the Emperor's Children as their Perfect Fortress during the Great Crusade, seized for Horus in the Heresy and retaken when Corax's Raven Guard broke its garrison.
A T'au sept world in Segmentum Ultima, abandoned for reasons the Earth Caste has never explained, its unsettling readings and green-tinged sun leaving a lingering mark on those who hail from it.

An ocean world in Segmentum Ultima, home to the Emperor's Spears Chapter, its scattered island chains and ice rings surrounding a planet whose tribal clans regard the Astartes as spirits who steal away their young.
A hive world of Segmentum Ultima that furnishes regiments for the Astra Militarum, noted chiefly in passing Imperial Guard rosters rather than any extended chronicle.
An Imperial world of Segmentum Ultima that fell into rebellion, quelled in 998.M41 when the Doom Legion's 4th Company intervened at a heavy cost in civilian lives.

A Nurgle-claimed daemon world in Segmentum Ultima, overrun after the Word Bearers honored an old pact with a Great Unclean One and drove its Adepta Sororitas garrison into full evacuation before plague and Plaguebearers finished the conquest.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, present in Imperial registries but absent from any deeper archive record.
A Necron tomb world in Segmentum Pacificus, one of the countless crypts where a sleeping dynasty waits out the eons beneath the surface.
A world of Segmentum Obscurus that holds the fortress-monastery of the Relictors Chapter.
A world once contested by Eldar and Death Guard forces during the Great Crusade, later annihilated when the Word Bearers broke its wardings and loosed the daemons sealed within.
A world of Segmentum Ultima swallowed by the Malfactus Warp Storm since the opening of the Great Rift, stranded on the far side of the Imperium Nihilus.

A hive world on the fringe of Segmentum Solar whose thirteen cities were infiltrated by the Genestealer cult known as the Hivecult, with only Hive Tharanex holding out until the Imperium crushed the resulting uprising.
A war-torn world of Segmentum Pacificus struck by Hive Fleet Leviathan in 997.M41, its fate afterward never confirmed.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Obscurus, noted in Imperial records with little further detail preserved.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its history largely absent from the surviving archive.
The homeworld of the Genesis Chapter, lying northwest of Ultramar, where an annual feast for the dead sends paper lanterns drifting skyward in memory of the fallen.
An Imperial astropathic relay station in Segmentum Ultima, maintaining psychic contact between Terra and worlds far beyond easy reach.
An Adeptus Astartes homeworld in Segmentum Ultima, standing as fortress-monastery ground for its resident Chapter.

An industrial world on the Imperium's border with the T'au Empire, overrun during the Second Sphere Expansion and marked by the Koloth Gorge Massacre before the Black Templars reclaimed it in the Nimbosa Crusade.
A cluster of daemon worlds in Segmentum Tempestus, claimed long ago by the warp and left largely undocumented beyond that fact.
A hive world of the Sabbat Worlds in Segmentum Pacificus, seized by Chaos and among the first targets struck when the Sabbat Worlds Crusade began in 765.M41.
An active war zone in Ultima Segmentum's Imperium Sanctus, close to the edge of the Great Rift. The Inquisition keeps most records of the fighting there sealed.
An agri world of the Cinerus Maleficum region within the Koronus Expanse, its harvests sustaining Imperial outposts along that distant frontier.
A Deathwatch watch fortress in Segmentum Solar. Its kill teams have struck the ork world Calverna time and again, at heavy cost on both sides.

An agri world settled in 890.M30 during the Great Crusade, its oceans and volcanic archipelagos long feeding the Imperium. Occupied early in the Horus Heresy, it was freed in what became known as the Liberation of Numinal.
A world overrun by a Genestealer Cult in Segmentum Ultima. The archive holds little more than that grim classification.

Formerly home to the Astral Knights, who were shattered fighting a Necron World Engine and ceded their monastery to the Sable Swords. A Novamarines rescue from a mutant horde later bound the Swords in a debt of honor.
A Hold World of the Greater Thurian League deep in the Galactic Core, seat of the Kindred of Vortun and birthplace of the renowned Kahl Uthar.
A world of Ultramar during the Great Crusade and Horus Heresy, once the seat of Tetrarch Valentus Dolor. Guilliman folded it back into the realm in M42, though its former standing was never restored.
A feudal world in Segmentum Pacificus that levies troops for the Imperial Guard. Beyond that recruiting duty, little of its history survives in the record.

An Adeptus Astartes homeworld ruined in late M41 by an Alaitoc reprisal raid. After the Great Rift opened, hauntings spread across the world before Huron Blackheart's Red Corsairs stole its orbiting star fort.

A Cardinal World consumed by Hive Fleet Kraken at the outset of the Second Tyrannic War. Canoness Praxedes slew the invading Hive Tyrant at the cost of her life, buying time for the Ecclesiarchy's evacuation.
A long-abandoned world of Segmentum Solar near Terra, seized by Chaos forces during the Indomitus Crusade for its warp nexus. Fleet Tertius later drove out the invaders and reclaimed the system.
An uncharted world in Segmentum Ultima, its record limited to a name on Imperial star charts.

A Fallen Knight World near the Siren's Storm warp storm, its Dread Keep held by a Chaos-corrupted Knight house. Cultists and fallen nobles there vie to raise ever grander shrines to the Ruinous Powers.
The oldest Cardinal World, second in sanctity only to Terra and once the diocese of Ecclesiarch Benedin IV. It hosts the Synod Ministra and the Adepta Sororitas' Convent Sanctorum.
An unlisted world in Segmentum Obscurus, its details absent from the surviving archive.
A feral world of the Pale Stars where pre-Imperial tribal culture endured into M31. It was the site of a Badab War boarding action against the Lamenters' strike cruiser Dawn Reaper.
A system on the western fringe of Segmentum Obscurus, sparsely documented in surviving Imperial records.
A Custodes star fort keeping watch over the Sol System, one of several void bastions the Ten Thousand hold in guard of Terra's approaches.
A hive world where the Assault on the Hellspire threw back a Chaos invasion, with Imperial Guard regiments and Space Marines from seven Chapters, including Blood Angels led by Commander Dante.

A former Imperial forge world in Segmentum Tempestus, publicly abandoned to orkish raiders but secretly seized by the Dark Mechanicum. Corrupted tech-priests bored into its core to widen the nearby warp rift called the Siren's Storm.

Home to the Iron Hounds, a loyalist Chapter descended from the Ultramarines line and known for hunting enemies to the last kill. Their fate turned grim when Nurgle's followers overran the nearby Scourge Stars.
Brought into Ultramar during the Great Crusade of the early 31st millennium by the war engines of Legio Praesagius. Little beyond that conquest survives in the archive.

A Ratling world in Segmentum Obscurus that once held the warp-tainted relic called the Eye of Night, worshipped as a god before Imperial contact. Chaos raiders later besieged and overran it seeking the artefact, killing millions ahead of the Gothic War.
Fortress world of the Praetors of Orpheus, a Second Founding successor Chapter of the Ultramarines, whose monastery burrows through a subterranean warren called the Labyrinthe of Orphia.
A Necron tomb world in Segmentum Ultima, claimed by the Hyrekh Dynasty and sealed against the living until its slumbering legions rouse again.
Counted among the Sabbat Worlds along the fringe of Segmentum Pacificus, within the cluster of systems known as the Newfound Trailing Group. Its own history beyond that listing is thinly recorded.
A Chapter world in Segmentum Obscurus, home to the Legion of Night, whose armoured columns of Rhinos and Razorbacks strike fast and withdraw before the enemy can regroup.
Recorded as an Adeptus Astartes world in Segmentum Ultima, home to the Red Legion Chapter. Beyond that lineage, little of its own character survives in the archive.

The sixth moon of the gas giant Paragon VI, ruled by a manufacturing aristocracy of a hundred fifty Clan Families. Its industrial output rivals a hive world's, and it feeds recruits to both the Astra Militarum and the Emperor's Hands Chapter.

A Mechanicum forge world that hosted rival Titan Legions during the Horus Heresy, its spaceport at Paramar Nexus fought over twice by loyalist and traitor forces including the Legio Gryphonicus and Legio Mortis.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, its census entry blank beyond bare coordinates.
An Ultramarines training world within Ultramar that fell to Nurgle's forces under Mortarion during the Plague Wars, until Roboute Guilliman broke Ku'gath's hold on its capital, Hecaton.
Once home to Imperial orbital docking yards wrecked by a Night Lords raid during the Thramas Crusade, now catalogued as a dead world in Segmentum Ultima.
Little else is recorded of this Segmentum Ultima world beyond its unclassified status in Imperial archives.
One of the moons orbiting Varsavia, given wholly to the funerary rites of the Silver Skulls, its surface covered in tombs holding silver urns shaped like the skulls of each Brother's slayer.
Homeworld of the Kroot, a jungle world of towering jagga forests where the warrior Anghkor Prok bound his people to the T'au Empire at the Oathstone on the Plain of Bones.
A Necron tomb world of the Mephrit Dynasty, lying dormant in Segmentum Ultima alongside its dynastic twin, Qyrakotosh.

An Adeptus Mechanicus research station where Iron Hands warriors clashed with a Death Guard task force under Calas Typhon during the Horus Heresy, both hunting a captive alien intelligence held within.
A dead world in Segmentum Ultima, its scant Imperial Guard and Death Guard chronicle entries offering little beyond that bare classification.

A permanent warp storm scars this stretch of Ultima Segmentum, bleeding raw immaterium into real space along its rim. Worlds near the edge, such as forbidden Locrus, lie exposed to daemonic incursions born of the storm.

An unclassified world of Ultima Segmentum whose surface is little more than shifting tectonic plates and open magma. Its population survives on isolated plateaus that range from farmland to mining outposts.

A derelict pre-human megastructure drifting through Segmentum Pacificus, large enough to hold a thin atmosphere within its ancient hull. It was shattered by a chain of nuclear blasts in 992.M41, the ruinous climax of the Perun Cross Incident.
A forge world and Titan world of Segmentum Solar, home to the Legio Osedax Titan Legion. In the later Great Crusade it ranked as the Mechanicus's second most productive forge after Mars itself.

Once the seat of a Terran Administration mission governing the Pale Stars region, this world was swallowed whole by a warp storm during the Horus Heresy. By the 41st Millennium it resurfaced as the forbidden world Phargos Hex.

An industrial world of Segmentum Tempestus in the Uhulis Sector, tithed for millions of Imperial Guard recruits every year. Its regiments have been levied to crush uprisings across neighboring systems.
A dead world of Segmentum Ultima, stripped of atmosphere and native life. Little else survives in the archive beyond its bleak classification.
An agri-world of Segmentum Obscurus, one among the Imperium's countless farm planets feeding the wider sector. Beyond that, the archive holds no further detail.

A Ramilies-class station anchoring the asteroid-choked Diomedea Stella system in Segmentum Obscurus, garrisoned by a small Naval detachment under a resident governor. Independent miners and the Prospectors Guild work the surrounding fields.

An artificial planetoid orbiting the world of Aestellica, raised late in the Great Crusade as an Imperialis Armada facility. Today it holds the Gothic Sector capital and Battlefleet Gothic's headquarters in Segmentum Obscurus.

An Imperial Navy waystation on the border of the Drusus Marches, founded in the fortieth millennium as an outpost against ships lost beyond the frontier. Trade with the Halo Stars later eclipsed its military purpose.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, noted in Imperial records but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
Once a feral night-world of tribal cannibal clans and the homeworld of the Mortifactors Chapter, Posul was later stripped of life, a dead world left in Segmentum Tempestus.
A supply world tied to the forge world Hypnoth, stripped of life during a Necron raid, and now claimed by the Sautekh Dynasty in Segmentum Ultima.
A desert death world, home to the Sons of Guilliman Chapter, an Ultramarines successor long dogged by rumors of Chaos taint and codex-breaking conduct.

Pranagar was home to the Sky Sentinels Chapter until it fell to the forces of Magnus the Red during the 13th Black Crusade; the annihilated Chapter's world became a domain of the Thousand Sons' primarch.
Garrisoned and dug in around the Abraxis Citadel, Prassium is an Imperial fortress world holding a line in Segmentum Ultima.
A death world in Segmentum Ultima, recorded among the Imperium's most hostile environments but sparsely documented beyond that.
A Custodian Guard star-keep watching the void between Sol and Hydraphur, one of the fortified stations screening the Throneworld's approaches.
A primary Deathwatch watch fortress in Segmentum Tempestus, self-sufficient with its own astropaths, Navigators and forges, from which Kill Teams are dispatched against xenos threats.
An active Imperial battlefield in Ultima Segmentum, presently sealed under Inquisitorial embargo that keeps most facts of the conflict from the wider record.
Homeworld of the Hawk Lords Chapter, a fortress world in Segmentum Ultima that raises Space Marines for the Imperium's wars.
A feudal domain settled by the Knights of House Terryn, seized by the Iron Warriors after Lord Admiral Vaen's flagship crashed there and his troops broke the local knightly houses.
Home to a Space Marine Chapter whose Imperial records have been purged, though surviving heraldry ties the world to the Rainbow Warriors.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, listed in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
An unclaimed world within the Pale Stars, marked as the point where the Blood Angels commander Serron broke off his pursuit of the Alpha Legion during the Horus Heresy.
A small Imperial research outpost in the Gothic Sector, overrun by Night Lords raiders searching for a Warp-forged relic later used by Abaddon against the Blackstone Fortresses.
A Black Templars crusade fleet led by Marshal Holgher, sent to guard Ecclesiarchy worlds near the Great Rift and now mired in a grinding war against the Szarekhan Dynasty.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, recorded in Imperial registries but otherwise absent from the archive.
An asteroid mining colony in Segmentum Obscurus, holding the northern approach to the Nachmund Gauntlet.
An Imperial world assailed by the T'au Empire in the late 41st millennium, where invasion gave way to an uneasy ceasefire aimed at a common Tyranid hive fleet bearing down on both sides.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its designation entered in Imperial registries with no further account preserved in the archive.

A feral member of the Cerada Trinity, paired with the civilised Cerada Primus and mining colony Cerada Secundus east of Numinal, a cluster long harassed by raiders and struck by traitor Mechanicum forces during the Horus Heresy.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, present in the census rolls but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
An agri-world of Segmentum Ultima, its harvests recorded in Administratum tithe ledgers though little else of its history survives.

An Imperial world in Segmentum Ultima whose Astra Militarum regiments answered the call of the Indomitus Crusade, later listed among worlds beset by a genestealer infestation despite its role as a Guard recruiting ground.
A tomb world of the Necron Mephrit Dynasty, whose overlords once mastered the art of igniting stars into supernovae and still arm their warriors with weapons drawing on captive suns.
A dead world of Segmentum Obscurus, listed among the Imperium's lifeless holdings with no further record surviving in the archive.

A hive world overrun by Hive Fleet Kraken despite defenses raised with help from the Scythes of the Emperor, later retaken by the Astra Militarum and left a ruined war world plagued by Tyranid remnants and cultist uprisings.
A dead world of Segmentum Ultima, its ruin noted in Imperial surveys though no further detail remains on file.
Homeworld of the Red Talons Chapter, a taciturn Space Marine successor descended from Leman Russ's line, garrisoned deep within Segmentum Tempestus.

A verdant Knight World where House Cadmus rules from Golem Keep, the only high ground clear of the planet's rampant forests. Abhuman tribes still roam the woodland below, spared by old custom rather than mercy.
A Death World on the fringe of the Koronus Expanse, hostile enough that only the hardiest expeditions dare chart its wilds.

Knight World and seat of House Cerberan until the Iron Warriors of Warsmith Ghanshor overran it after the Great Rift tore open. The last loyal knight, Hekhtur, swore the Freeblade's oath and led the resistance that followed.
Once the crown Knight World of the Reductus Sector and seat of House Aramos, until Hive Fleet Leviathan overran it and left it a dead world in Segmentum Tempestus.
A hive world of Segmentum Solar that doubles as an Imperial Guard recruiting ground. Little else survives in the archive.
A Fallen Knight World near the Somnium Stars warp storm, its noble house long since corrupted by Chaos and its keep given over to cultists and traitor knights.

What remains of the craftworld Biel-Tan after a daemonic assault during the 13th Black Crusade shattered its wraithbone core in 999.M41, scattering the once-mighty vessel into a drifting fleet of fragments.
A dying war world of unknown segmentum standing, where the Death Korps of Krieg's 472nd Siege Regiment held the line against the T'au rather than withdraw.
A civilized world of Segmentum Solar, noted in Imperial record for the purge of its corrupt naval aristocracy, an early case worked by Inquisitor Gholic Ren-Sar Valinov.

An Ultima-Founding Chapter of Primaris Space Marines, clad in black armour marked by a white cobra's head, deployed against the Ork infestation of the Kebban Sub-sector and later fought in the Octarius War.
A tomb world of the Sautekh Dynasty sleeping beneath Segmentum Ultima, one among the countless Necron vaults scattered across the galaxy, its dynastic lords awaiting a signal to rise again.
An unclassified world recorded in Segmentum Ultima, its nature and population left undocumented in the archive.

A forge world second only to Mars in output, home to the Legio Crucius Titans and famed for its plasma weaponry. Loyalist during the Horus Heresy, it survived a Dark Mechanicus assault at ruinous cost to its factories.

Once homeworld to the White Consuls Chapter, this daemon world fell to the Death Guard's Lords of Silence and a Word Bearers warband after the Great Rift tore open, remade into a fetid mirror of the Plague Planet.

An Imperial world near the southern approach to the Nachmund Gauntlet, drawn uncomfortably close to the Great Rift, sustaining its population through the industry expected of a civilised world in Segmentum Obscurus.

Stranded within the Dark Imperium after the Great Rift tore open, this world saw its people butchered by the Ten Thousand Eyes warband in a scheme to ensnare the returned Lion El'Jonson, before joining the Lion's Protectorate.
A T'au sept world in Segmentum Ultima, one of the Greater Good's settled domains, otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A feral world in Segmentum Ultima, its tribes and terrain otherwise unrecorded in the archive.

A radiation-blasted death world that fed recruits to the Blood Angels Legion, whose feral population kept its sanity at Signus Prime while others succumbed to the Red Thirst. World Eaters invaders were later driven off after a long guerrilla war.
A daemon world claimed by the Architect of Fate somewhere in Segmentum Obscurus. Little else survives in the archive.

An oceanic world of the Farsight Enclaves, its surface formed from vast globes of liquid orbiting the star Emon'hai. Tau Earth Caste engineers once triggered a seismic tsunami here to wipe out an invading Ork fleet.

An asteroid monastery of the Adeptus Ministorum overrun by Hive Fleet Kraken in the 41st Millennium. Its monks poisoned their own ecosystem with a lethal rotweed rather than let the Tyranids consume them alive.

A once-quiet oceanic mining world seized by the Tau Empire in the early 42nd Millennium. A psychic affliction tied to the Fallen Angel Gohorael's schemes drew the Dark Angels into a desperate fight to save its human population.
A world the Imperium counted of negligible strategic value, yet still defended by the Night Reapers against an assault of Chaos Space Marines and mutant hordes.

A forge world cut off from the Imperium when the Hadex Anomaly warp storm erupted, its Quorum of Magi falling to the Ruinous Powers soon after. The Dark Mechanicus now rule it as the Furnace Lord's stronghold, arming Chaos warbands across the region.

An agricultural world of long standing, home before the Age of Apostasy to the militant all-female cult known as the Daughters of the Emperor. Genestealer infestation has since taken root beneath its fields.
A frontier world of Segmentum Obscurus, recorded in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.

An Ecclesiarchy world lost in 437.M36, when Mortarion led the Death Guard across its surface in a single day, following an Ork assault that devoured the living and the dead alike. It has festered as a daemon world of the Plague God ever since.

Homeworld of the White Templars Chapter, a land of high peaks once roamed by techno-barbarian tribes. Lord Commander Leontus named it an Anchor World during the Fourth Tyrannic War, its wilderness reforged into fortress-cities and supply depots.
A war world in Segmentum Obscurus where a Genestealer Cult took root among the population long before open rebellion broke out.
A civilized world of the Sabbat Worlds cluster, its economy built on refinery hives, briefly overrun by a Chaos cult called the Kith after the crusade secured Balhaut.
One of the founding worlds of Ultramar, once ruled by a Tetrarch during the Great Crusade, later described by Roboute Guilliman as long devastated.
A mining world that turned traitor during the Horus Heresy, scoured by an Iron Hands orbital bombardment around 008.M31 and left a poisoned ruin ever since.

Seat of the necron Empire of the Severed, its legions left mindless after radiation storms erased the memories stored within their tomb. A rogue master program now drives them to absorb other tomb worlds.
The necron crownworld of the Sekemtar Dynasty in Segmentum Ultima, one of the ancient hubs of Necrontyr power that slept through the ages undisturbed.
The capital world of the Antimar Subsector in Segmentum Obscurus, governing the surrounding systems as a seat of Imperial administration.
A forge world that pledged itself to Horus during the Heresy and became a hell forge of the Dark Mechanicum, its barren surface given over to World Eaters daemon engines under Angron's command.

A world of Segmentum Ultima known for elixirs that sustain settlers across the Red Scar's Vitria and Cryptus systems. Blood Angels have studied a local bloodline here searching for a cure to the Black Rage.
Also called Savlar Penitens, a toxic penal world circled by dead moons whose hardened convicts are conscripted into the Imperial Guard as the Savlar Chem-Dogs.

An Ultima Segmentum world overrun by a Genestealer Cult, marked for quarantine by the Imperium. The Ork hulk Revenant Rex once drifted through its system, defeating every Astartes attempt to purge it, including the Aurora Chapter's.
A warp storm charted in Segmentum Ultima, feared by navigators for currents that shift without warning. Little else survives in the archive.

The Alpha Legion overran Seer's Rest, crushing its last Imperial Guard garrison and dosing the air with a gas that woke latent psychic gifts in its people. Thousands of comatose psykers were taken before the world was declared a Daemon World.

Seidon lay at the heart of the ancient Necrontyr expansion, its tomb complexes built to launch a colony ship every thirty three weeks toward the wider galaxy. The Stasis Docks of Seidon still disgorge invasion fleets, aimed at coordinates lost to time.

Semmel is a ghost world of the Vidar Sector on the Eastern Fringe, a stretch of the Imperium worn thin by constant raids. In 918.M41 a Blood Angels strike force sent to its aid vanished, wiped out by Eldar and an unidentified foe.

Sepheris Secundus is a feudal mining world of the Calixis Sector, its serfs bound since birth to hack ore under an absolute monarch, Queen Lachryma III. Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn was born into that servitude before the Ordo Xenos took him.
An Adeptus Astartes world in Segmentum Obscurus, home ground to a Space Marine Chapter. Little else survives in the archive.

Septum Verda is an Imperial industrial world in the southern reaches of the Nachmund Gauntlet, its factories given over entirely to war production. Little of daily life there is recorded beyond the output of its foundries.

Seraphina was cleared of a longstanding Ork infestation by the Space Wolves during the Wheel of Fire campaign of the Great Crusade. Primarch Leman Russ received the Dionysian Spear in recognition of that victory.

The Sessec System was overrun by the Red Corsairs in 999.M41. During the Thirteenth Black Crusade it was fought over again when Huron Blackheart's warhost pushed out of the Maelstrom, met there by the White Scars, Storm Lords, and Doom Legion.

Seven Dooms names a cluster of long dead suns in the Heathen Stars region of the Koronus Expanse, their light extinguished by an ancient calamity and their remains still wrapped in the gases of that destruction.
Shedim is a gas giant whose dense atmosphere once hid ambush fleets during the Betrayal at Grief and, in earlier centuries, warships fighting through the War in the Shedim Drifts.

Sherilax served the Imperium as a pilgrim world until the year 703 of the fortieth millennium, when a rebellion against a harsh governor opened the way for Slaanesh and its daemon Zarakynel to claim the population whole.
Shern manufactures war machines for the Adeptus Mechanicus at the northern gateway into the Nachmund Gauntlet, its output feeding the Imperium's defense of that contested passage.
Shoar is an ice bound world in the Coronid Deeps, where tribal clans cling to the coastal estuaries near the equator in warfare that has never ceased.
An unclassified site in Segmentum Ultima bearing the name of Elronhir, a hero of Eldar myth said to have driven humanity's ancestors from the stars. Little else is recorded.
A frozen world in Segmentum Ultima, catalogued among the Imperium's holdings but otherwise absent from surviving archive record.
Sidaiva lies among the Pale Stars, a mire sea world where Blood Angels and White Scars fought beside native inhabitants against the Alpha Legion during the Horus Heresy.

Sigma-Ulstari is a forge world in the Cordon Impenetra bordering Ork held Octarius, its factory spires rising from seas of toxic waste. Nearly overrun by Tyranids and Orks, it was saved by an Indomitus Crusade taskforce.

Signnus Prime once anchored the Signus Cluster's hive worlds, until the Horus Heresy saw the Greater Daemon Kyriss the Perverse seize it for Slaanesh, leaving it a daemon world remembered for endless dread.
A Space Marine Chapter homeworld within the Imperium Nihilus, sworn to defend Segmentum Obscurus's darkened reaches after the Cicatrix Maledictum split the galaxy.

A corrupted forge world of the Dark Mechanicum, hidden within a region called the Silent Abyss and ruled by the Sepktraal Cult's dark magi. Its foundries are blamed for the dreaded Decimator daemon engines.

A warp storm born during the Noctis Aeterna that tore through the Soelich Sub-sector. Dark Mechanicum agents once tried to steer it toward Terra itself, a plot broken by Magos Xu Kroll's Reclamation Fleet.
One of the quarantined worlds of the Nexaris System, sealed off since M39 when the system's sun turned black and its planets fell to corruption.
An Imperial world whose population rose in rebellion, put down in 997.M41 by Astra Militarum regiments from Pintax, Tallarn and Luther McIntyre at heavy cost.
A hive world of the Nachmund Sector, its billions packed into towering stacks on the fringe of the Imperium's eastern reaches.
A half frozen feudal world whose people have only just climbed out of a primitive age, still unaware of the wider Imperium around them.
A Necron tomb world of the Sautekh Dynasty, ruled by Overlord Szaron under Imotekh the Stormlord. In 798.M41 an Alaitoc war host led by Farseer Starbane besieged its crypts and never returned.

The city sized flagship of Szarekh, the Silent King, aboard which he exiled himself after the ruin of the Biotransference. He recalled it from isolation only when the Tyranid hive fleets began to threaten his people.
An active Imperial warzone near Quisto'rol in the Imperium Nihilus, its details sealed under Inquisitorial classification.

Known as the Planet of the Sorcerers, this daemon world became the new home of the Thousand Sons after Tizca was torn from Prospero and set down upon it. Magnus's obsidian Tower of the Cyclops dominates its ever shifting wastes.

A world swallowed by a warp storm for centuries before reappearing in 400.M41, its cities reduced to ruin and its people gone. Salvagers still comb its shattered data engines for illicit fragments known as flects.
A dead world adrift in Ultima Segmentum, its history lost to the archive save for its name.

A missionary world where a Deathwatch kill team once purged a hidden genestealer cult, only for a Tyranid hive fleet to finish what the xenos started. Its Isle later became a battleground against the renegade Crimson Slaughter.
A missionary world undone from within before Hive Fleet Leviathan ever arrived. The Lictor known as Deathleaper stalked its defenders and broke Cardinal Salem's sanity, leaving the world easy prey.

A warp conduit torn open by accident when the T'au Fourth Sphere Expansion's slipstream drives misfired en masse, linking Tau sept space to the Imperium's Nem'yar Atoll. Both ends are now fortified strongholds.

A warp conduit torn open by accident when the T'au Fourth Sphere Expansion's slipstream drives misfired en masse, linking Tau sept space to the Imperium's Nem'yar Atoll. Both ends are now fortified strongholds.
A warp storm named for the Chaos Primarch Magnus, cataloged among the countless tempests that scar the Ultima Segmentum's stellar routes.

A warp storm that erupted during the Age of Apostasy, swallowing the fleet Goge Vandire sent against Sebastian Thor's rebels. It still rages over the Clax system millennia later, remembered by some as a sign of the Emperor's wrath.
A warp storm that marooned a World Eaters warband, led by the Dreadnought Dharkallon, on the feral world of Cylenia for over a thousand years before finally abating.
Homeworld of the Iron Hawks Chapter, standing in the Imperium Nihilus near the roiling Somnium Stars warp storm.
Three warp storms that tore open across the Caradryad Sector, plunging its worlds into madness and revolt until the Grey Knights answered the breach.
A world remembered for the Eldar Phoenix Lord Maugan-Ra's solitary stand against a splinter of Hive Fleet Leviathan, which he broke alone.

An ancient human enclave brought into the Imperium during the Great Crusade and fed by the forge world Ohmn-Mat. When the Horus Heresy began, its people spurned the Warmaster's offers and faced a Dark Compliance for their loyalty.

Three knight worlds ring the forge world of Stryken Primus, home to eleven knightly houses whose finest champions form the standing guard called the Order of Iron. The system's foundries pour munitions toward the defense of the Cadian Gate.

A forge moon orbiting a ringed gas giant, ruled by the Xenarites, tech priests who study forbidden xenos devices despite wider Mechanicus taboo. Its workshops are prized for producing some of the finest gun barrels and propellant in the Imperium.
A dead world in Segmentum Ultima, its surface silent and unrecorded beyond the census entry.
An ork-held world in Segmentum Ultima, claimed by the greenskins and otherwise absent from wider record.
An industrial world in Segmentum Ultima, its factories logged in the census but its history otherwise unwritten.

Once the home of House Khymere, this knight world fell to corruption unleashed by the Great Rift. The Indomitus Crusade purged its surface, leaving it a graveyard while the fallen knights linger beyond the rift.

A hollow relic world whose vaults held stasis-locked treasures under guard. Raiders who broke its seals woke a daemon prince entombed inside, and only Grey Knights Terminators could beat back what followed.
A forge world of the Calixis Sector's Malfian Subsector, one of two Mechanicus-held worlds bearing the Synford name that supply the sector's armories.
Homeworld of the Sons of Medusa since the 39th millennium, an Iron Hands successor chapter that raised its fortress monastery here.

Gifted to the Deathwatch by the Ultramarines, this fortress is their primary training ground and capital, raising kill teams drawn largely from the Ultramarines, Scythes of the Emperor, and Lamenters to hunt the Tyranid threat.

An ocean world of Ultramar whose single continent, Glaudor, saw the Ultramarines drive off a great ork invasion in the Age of Rebirth. Birthplace of Cato Sicarius, it is now homeworld to the Void Tridents chapter.
A warp storm churning through Segmentum Tempestus, marked on charts but left unexplained in the archive.
A dead world in Segmentum Ultima once home to a Space Marine chapter, remembered chiefly for its role during the Tyrannic Wars.
One of several star keeps ringing the Sol System, held by the Adeptus Custodes alongside sister stations such as Eyrie Prime and Oracle Maximus.
Former homeworld of the Brazen Claws chapter, overrun by a daemonic horde in 955.M41. The survivors fled into the Eye of Terror on a crusade of vengeance and were seldom seen again.
A tomb world claimed by the Ogdobekh Dynasty, its necron legions sleeping beneath the surface until called to wake.

Ruled by the matriarchal Knight House Thorne, this world sent its nobles to fight beside the Iron Hands against the traitor Legio Abominator at Medusa. Its Baroness is said to be the finest duellist in the galaxy.
Homeworld of the Jade Scorpions Space Marine Chapter, within Segmentum Tempestus.
A dead world in Segmentum Obscurus, its atmosphere long stripped away and little more than its name preserved in the census.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Tempestus, present on the charts without further record.

An agri world of Ultramar, its domed cities wringing food and water from parched land alongside its sister worlds Quintarn and Masali. It endured ork raids and later daemonic incursion during the Invasion of Ultramar.
A hive world plagued by a stubborn ork infestation, where Raven Guard operatives spent two years working behind enemy lines feeding targeting data before the push to retake it.
A hive world of the Dhobash System, recorded among its neighboring worlds in Imperial cartography.

A world of the Sabbat Worlds cluster where failed Chaos counterattacks let Imperial forces regain the initiative on the Khan Group flank during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. Centuries later, Mechanicus adepts still search its ruins for lost technology.
A frontier sept of the T'au Empire in Segmentum Ultima, valued for its gem trade and defended time and again against alien raiders by some of the finest Breacher squads in the Empire.
The first colony sept the T'au Empire ever founded beyond its homeworld, home to vast orbital docks and the largest fleet anchorage of the Air Caste.
During the Horus Heresy, Ultramarines under Chapter Master Arceas Odinathus ambushed and wiped out a World Eaters garrison holding this world in northern Segmentum Ultima.
An unclassified world in Segmentum Ultima, its census entry bare of further record.
A warp storm raging through Segmentum Solar, swallowing shipping lanes without warning.
A webway gate charted in Segmentum Obscurus, its far end never mapped by Imperial cartographers.
A warp storm tracked drifting through Segmentum Ultima, its course too erratic for astropaths to predict.
A warp storm recorded over Segmentum Ultima, named for the darkness that reportedly swallows any vessel caught within it.
A port world at the gateway to the Askellon frontier in Segmentum Obscurus, traditionally the first waypoint traders reach and once a prosperous centre of commerce for the sector.
Once the most powerful system of the Lirac Sub in Segmentum Ultima, until Be'lakor's forces tore its Knight World free during the Charadon Campaign and dragged it beyond the Great Rift.
A Necron crownworld of the Oruscar Dynasty, guarding the Celestial Orrery, an ancient device whose lights map every star in the galaxy and can trigger their destruction if extinguished.
A webway gate logged within Segmentum Tempestus, its passage older than most records the Imperium keeps.
A webway gate in Segmentum Obscurus, marked on old star charts by the wrecks left scattered around its threshold.
A webway gate in Segmentum Obscurus, surrounded by drifting hulks that gave the site its grim name.
A webway gate within Segmentum Solar, noted in the archive only by its position and the traffic that occasionally vanishes there.
A webway gate in Segmentum Tempestus, its name hinting at eldar activity the Imperium has never confirmed.
A warp storm coiling through Segmentum Solar, its spiral pattern making it one of the more predictable hazards charted in the region.
A warp storm in Segmentum Ultima known to swallow entire convoys without leaving wreckage behind.
A warp storm twisting through Segmentum Ultima, its readings said to repeat and distort astropathic signals for years afterward.
A Knight World in Segmentum Solar fallen to Chaos, its ruling house corrupted and its populace given over to depraved cults under the Dark Powers.
A webway gate in Segmentum Ultima, its threshold said to be carved with wards from a language no living scholar can read.
A warp storm burning across Segmentum Solar, its crimson glow visible to astropaths long before any ship draws near.
Another warp rift bearing the same name in Segmentum Solar, distinct in the charts only by its coordinates and the ships it has claimed.
A webway gate in Segmentum Solar, named for the perpetual dimness that surrounds its threshold in every recorded sighting.
Charted as a daemon world adrift in Segmentum Ultima's warp tides, with no further record surviving in the archive.
A warp storm churns through Segmentum Ultima at this position, its origin and duration unrecorded by Imperial cartographers.
One of the webway gates charted within Segmentum Ultima, its far terminus unmapped by any Imperial survey.
Imperial cartographers list this as a webway gate in Segmentum Ultima, though its builders and purpose remain unknown.

Known to Imperial xenologists as a Tesseract Labyrinth grown to the size of a world, this Necron construct in Segmentum Solar is a prison so absolute that even the Necrons refuse to draw on what it holds.
Old star charts mark a persistent warp storm here in Segmentum Ultima, avoided by navigators for reasons no longer preserved.
Segmentum Solar's charts note a warp storm at this position, listed without further annotation.
Not a fixed fortress but a roving fleet maintained by the Deathwatch, the Onyx Patrol hunts nomadic Aeldari craftworlds across Segmentum Solar.
Segmentum Obscurus charts include this webway gate among the region's known crossings, with nothing more recorded of it.
This webway gate in Segmentum Ultima appears on old survey charts, though no expedition is recorded as having passed through it.
A vast permanent warp rift separating the Calixis Sector from the Koronus Expanse, named for a psychic wail only sorcerers and the gifted can perceive.
Segmentum Ultima's charts carry this webway gate under that name, its passage undocumented beyond the label.
Named for Morai-Heg, the Aeldari goddess of fate also called the Crone, this webway gate in Segmentum Solar bears the mark of her cult.
Segmentum Obscurus records this warp storm by name alone, its shifting arms the apparent source of its title.
Another warp storm troubles Segmentum Obscurus here, its title hinting at some daemonic patron long lost to record.
Whatever this webway gate in Segmentum Tempestus once guarded, only its name survives in the archive.
This warp storm in Segmentum Obscurus is one of many such phenomena that make the region treacherous to navigate.
This webway crossing in Segmentum Ultima is noted on old charts, its structure and far end lost to the record.
Discovered during the Macharian Crusade as an Imperial desert world, Thoth was later declared Amundi Heretica and scoured by virus bombs after its people turned to witchcraft.
Once the capital of its system, Thranix fell when Word Bearers forces branded its moons with burning eight-pointed stars and daemonic possession spread across the world.
A hive world liberated during the Great Crusade and later riddled with Genestealer infiltration, Thranx was ultimately subjected to Exterminatus and cleansed by virus bombing.
A forge world of Segmentum Ultima maintained by the Adeptus Mechanicus, its output and history otherwise absent from the archive.
Homeworld of the Sons of Galathor Chapter, Throne of Galat lies near the Malfactus Warp storm and has weathered repeated invasion in the era after the Great Rift.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, its nature left undetermined in the surviving census.
Recorded only by its coordinates in Segmentum Ultima, Thun carries no further classification in the archive.
An unclassified world drifting in Segmentum Pacificus, noted in the census without further detail.
A forge world of the Imperium and home to the Titan Legion Legio Solaria, held by orks for much of the 41st millennium until the Indomitus Crusade drew it back into the war against the Great Rift.
A crystalline world of the Farsight Enclaves, its jagged crust stripped away by the Earth Caste until only a smooth, glasslike surface remained, dotted with Air Caste orbitals.
A T'au sept world renowned for its Ethereal diplomats and alien embassies, producing commanders who favor patience and calculation over the front line charge.
A hive world of the Sithoza System whose planetary leadership fell wholesale to a Chaos cult, purged only after Space Marine chapters besieged its factorums and broke the heretics from within.
An Adeptus Astartes homeworld in Segmentum Ultima, recruiting ground of the Avenging Sons Chapter.

A Necron crownworld of the Dyvanakh Dynasty. Its warriors woke groggy from millennia of hibernation, drawing the attention of a neighboring Imperial forge world, and only after years of siege warfare did they finally drive the invaders off world.
A feudal world of Segmentum Ultima, its knights and peasant levies noted in Imperial census but little chronicled beyond that.
Known among raiders as the Raider's Moon, this Necron tomb world in Segmentum Tempestus regularly launches raiding fleets against the resource-rich Imperial world of Mandal nearby.
Little beyond its position in Segmentum Obscurus survives of Treconandal in the archive.
A fortress world and capital of the Rhodior System, standing guard as one of the Metalican Gates ringing the forge world Metalica in the Charadon Sector.

An isolated forge world on Ultima Segmentum's eastern fringe, defended by the Titan Legion Legio Victorum and a dozen Knight Houses against a Tyranid splinter fleet, a Plague Fleet under Typhus, and a Daemon incursion.

A forge world of orbital cities whose capital, the Heptaligon, was built into the moon Momus. During the Horus Heresy its loyalist viceroy was usurped by a traitor magos, delivering the system to Horus's fleet.
An ice world whose tribes hunt across frozen wastes aboard colossal armored icebreakers, and whose winter warriors are drafted into the Astra Militarum as the Truskan Snowhounds.
A daemon world of the Askellon Sector lost long ago to the warp, where the Tzeentchian Daemon Prince Erravan the Schemer wages endless war against the followers of Khorne for its ruined surface.
Segmentum Pacificus holds Tunusk among its uncharted worlds, its nature otherwise unrecorded.
Tuonelia appears in Segmentum Ultima's survey rolls, its classification never filled in.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Solar, its details lost to whatever oversight left the record blank.
A Necron tomb world in Segmentum Tempestus once claimed by the Agdagath Dynasty, abandoned late in the 41st millennium after its own Flayed Ones overran the crypts.

A forest world laced with tunnels bored by giant magma-worms beneath its mountains and waterways. Word Bearers and Legio Infernus stormed it during the Horus Heresy but were driven off by loyalist Titan legions.

A shrine world marking the final conquest of the Macharian Crusade, dominated by the Great Column raised in memory of Lord Solar Macharius. Contact was lost when the Astronomican's light dimmed at the century's close.
Charted in Segmentum Ultima and named in old survey records, Ultra Mundanus otherwise left no mark on the archive.
A warp storm churning through Segmentum Ultima, its shifting currents swallowing whatever ships stray too close.
An Imperial world that stands watch among Terra's outer bastions. During the Blood Crusade, Craftworld Saim-Hann jetbike clans harried the Khornate host until the daemons abandoned the siege.
A ruined world within the Eye of Terror, home to the Tower of Silence where Abaddon the Despoiler recovered the daemon blade Drach'nyen during his first Black Crusade.
Once home to Eldar Exodite settlers, later claimed by the Imperium after the Crimson Fists broke a large Exodite force there in what became known as the Scourging of Uralek Prime.
An Exodite world of the Aeldari, sparsely documented beyond its listing among the scattered agrarian colonies these star faring farmers built outside the craftworlds.
A verdant Aeldari Maiden World famed for its thornwoods, until a Chaos incursion through a hidden webway portal left it ravaged and eventually claimed as a Daemon World.
A world bound to the Ruinous Powers of Change in Segmentum Obscurus, its fate as a Tzeentchian Daemon World recorded but little detailed in the surviving archive.
An Adeptus Mechanicus forge world in Segmentum Ultima, its furnaces and manufactoria feeding the Imperium's war effort with scant further record surviving.
An Imperial world settled near the northern approach to the Nachmund Gauntlet, its population living within reach of one of the Imperium's watched passages through the Great Rift.
A world overrun by the greenskin tribes of the Orks, somewhere among the countless worlds of Segmentum Ultima claimed by the endless Waaagh.
A world bound to the Adeptus Astartes in Segmentum Ultima, its role in raising or supplying a Chapter of Space Marines otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A frozen Imperial world whose people burrow beneath ancient ice sheets, best known as the origin of the famed Valhallan Ice Warrior regiments of the Astra Militarum.
A hell forge of the Belt of Iron that turned traitor during the Horus Heresy, its magi expelled and its population fled before Loyalist forces reached its abandoned halls.
A violent warp rift nicknamed Hell's Slingshot, capable of hurling ships across the Empyrean far faster than ordinary warp travel, at great risk to those who chart it.
A world of Segmentum Pacificus noted in the Imperial census, its history and character otherwise lost to the archive.

A hive forge world that stayed loyal through the Horus Heresy, weathering a brutal Traitor drop assault before liberation in the Great Scouring. Its foundries are known for artillery patterns like the Basilisk and Griffon.
An unclassified world recorded in Segmentum Obscurus, its history absent from the archive.

A harsh feral world of tribal warriors, split between a settled north and a savage south. Its finest fighters are recruited into the Silver Skulls, whose fortress monastery sits in its northern peaks.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Pacificus, present in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
A T'au sept world settled late in the Second Sphere Expansion, home to major Air Caste fleets and orbital defence platforms. Its Fire Caste trains for boarding actions and ship to ship combat.
Once the Imperial fortress world of Prefectia, guarding the strategic Dovar System, until it fell to the T'au under Commander Shadowsun during the Third Sphere Expansion and was renamed.
An Imperial world reduced to ruin by the Vaxhallian Genocides of 926.M41, when Chaos Space Marines of the Purge poisoned its crops and loosed plagues that killed over fourteen billion citizens.
A world liberated during the Great Crusade in the thirtieth millennium, remembered chiefly for the Titans of Legio Atarus that fought in its campaign.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, named on old campaign charts but otherwise unrecorded in the archive.

A civilized world in the Pahr Sector long valued for its bio-alchemical exports to the Officio Medicae. Genetic experiments by its own elite spawned the Twisted Helix, a Genestealer Cult that now rules in secret.
A forge world that hosts the Cyclopeans, a Titan Legion of the Collegia Titanica remembered for its campaign against the Iconoclastic Brotherhood in the War of Statues.
A maiden world in the Octarius Sector, once lush parkland shaped by the Aeldari before the Fall. It became a battleground between Ork and Tyranid forces and remains contested to this day.
A world in Segmentum Ultima listed as infested by a Genestealer Cult, its fate otherwise undocumented in the archive.
Home world of the Veradan Regulars, an Imperial Army regiment raised during the Great Crusade and remembered for their doomed stand against Ek'Lobia's hordes at Kiy-Buran.
A Nurgle claimed world of the Phantax Sector, used by the Death Guard as a base for slaving and trade, its plagues multiplying since the Great Rift tore open.

A fuel refining colony cluster on the Ultramar to Eastern Fringes warp route, forced into the Horus Heresy's traitor cause. Its Night Lords garrison blew the reactors rather than yield to an avenging Ultramarines fleet.
A remote system of the Coronid Deeps, linked by warp tunnel routes and noted mainly as an exploration outpost during the era of the Horus Heresy.
A storm wracked gas giant three light years from D'yanoi, where the insectoid Vespid carve their city nations into rock islands adrift in the violet stratosphere. Aligned with the T'au Empire.
A feral world on the rimward edge of the Antimar Subsector, whose hardy population produces an unusual number of psychic blanks and the ferocious mercenary bands known as the Vessorine Janissaries.
An Imperial world at the heart of the Vidar Sector on the Eastern Fringe, a domain under constant siege by xenos and daemons and ruled by self serving noble dynasties.
The ruling Cardinal World of the Lirac Sub-Sector, governed directly by the Ecclesiarchy with its surface given over to temples and shrines.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, recorded in the census but otherwise undocumented in the archive.
An unclassified world of Segmentum Obscurus, its history absent from the surviving archive.
An Imperial world whose Vintor 823rd regiment became notorious for being wiped out and re-raised under the same name nine times within a single decade.

A T'au sept world orbiting a binary star, its scorching climate matched only by its role as a major military centre of the empire. Its Pathfinders once lured an Ork invasion fleet into deadly plasma storms.
A volcanic world of the Farsight Enclaves, its geothermal power harnessed by the Earth Caste to run Fire Warrior academies and armament factories rivaling those of Vior'la itself.
A prosperous Agri World and hub for the mining stations of its moons and asteroids, drawing enough merchant shipping that locals joke nothing worth having is unavailable there.

Once the sandstorm scoured death world of Silica VII, until a solar flare in 165.M40 fused its wastes into black glass and left its rebuilt cities lined with razor sharp ruins. Later overrun by Hive Fleet Leviathan.
Charted only as an unclassified world in Segmentum Tempestus, with no further record surviving in Imperial archives.
Segmentum Obscurus holds Volscar as an unclassified entry, its details lost to incomplete Imperial cartography.
A verdant ocean world in Segmentum Ultima, ancestral seat of House Terryn's Questor Imperialis knights. Its Knights fought off a Horus Heresy assault three dozen strong.
Imperial cartographers list Volurilis as an unclassified world of Segmentum Ultima, with nothing more recorded.
Von Graubok's Reach appears in Segmentum Ultima star charts as an unclassified region, otherwise undocumented.
A civilized world of Segmentum Pacificus, seat of the Vordrast System, whose gas giant Othana V once drew a Custodes strike against Dark Eldar raiders.
Once a Knight World in Segmentum Ultima, until an Ork Waaagh in 544.M32 annihilated its Knight Houses Illius and Nero. The Necron Novokh Dynasty later struck the reclaimed world.
Former homeworld of the Crimson Castellans Chapter in Segmentum Ultima, abandoned after events that left the Chapter permanently fleet based.
A hive world of the Chalnath Expanse in Segmentum Ultima, torn between Tau annexation and the Pauper Princes Genestealer Cult during the M42 uprising that bears its name.
A warp storm in Segmentum Tempestus raised by the grieving seers of Craftworld Iyanden to banish their foes, briefly swallowing a splinter of Hive Fleet Kraken.
A forge world of Segmentum Solar shielded by the resource rich Mordan Belt, renowned for its own patterns of autopistols and aircraft though it never mastered plasma weapons.
Known as the Right Hand of Mars, the Segmentum Solar forge world nearest Armageddon is home to Legio Invigilata and briefly sided with the Dark Mechanicum during the Horus Heresy.
An industrial world of Segmentum Obscurus beyond the Eye of Terror, its harsh manufactoria supply the Imperium's arms while every firstborn son serves in the Vostroyan Firstborn regiments.
Site of an eighteen year rebellion and siege by traitor forces backed by the Alpha Legion, fought by the Death Korps of Krieg until the world was left ruined and its system placed under interdiction.

An Imperial world of rust and tech slums ruled by violent gang culture, whose warbands are drafted into the Astra Militarum as the Vresh Grenadiers, keeping their old gang markings on scavenged armour.
An Ork held world of Segmentum Tempestus, contested across millennia. Commissar Sebastian Yarrick once campaigned there and learned the greenskin tongue from a captive taken in battle.
A civilized world of Segmentum Ultima drawn into the internal war of the Badab sector, its name recorded among the battlegrounds where loyalist and rebel Space Marine forces clashed.
A Deathwatch fortress standing on the border of Segmentum Pacificus and Tempestus, housing Kill teams, astropaths, and a hoard of captured xenos relics kept for study.
An Ultima Segmentum world whose census entry survives in Imperial records, though little further detail about it has reached this archive.
A frigid moon barely able to sustain human life, brought into Imperial rule by the Night Lords during the Great Crusade and later emptied of its people during the Thramas Crusade, leaving only ruins.
A world of Segmentum Solar noted in Imperial census records, its deeper history otherwise absent from the archive.
The chapter world of the Subjugators, an Adeptus Astartes force that keeps its fortress monastery and draws its recruits from Segmentum Solar's own population.
A war world of Segmentum Ultima, its plains given over to unending conflict and its cities long since reduced to ash and rubble.
A dead world drifting in Segmentum Pacificus, stripped of atmosphere and life, noted only in passing within Imperial records.
A warp storm charted within Segmentum Ultima, its true origin and duration lost amid imprecise astropathic reports.
A hive world of the Eastern Fringe fought over during the Thramas Crusade, where the Night Haunter himself led the assault that broke its Imperial defenders.

An Imperial system in Segmentum Solar where the Space Wolves made a desperate stand against Abaddon's traitor fleet during the Horus Heresy, and where Eldar forces later drove leaderless Death Guard remnants from the stars.
A forge world under Adeptus Mechanicus rule in Segmentum Tempestus. Little beyond its classification survives in the archive.

A mysterious Necron construct on the fringe of Sautekh Dynasty territory in Segmentum Ultima, revealed after the Great Rift's opening to be a phase node of vast power, its glow untouched by the warpstorm Cerberax.

An Aeldari craftworld near the Eye of Terror, its wrights famed for artistry and titan scale war engines rather than wraithbone constructs, and rumored to guard a soul harvesting weapon never yet unleashed.
A T'au sept world of the Nem'yar Atoll, its flat circular landmasses hint at ancient terraforming, and its open plains now train the Fire Caste of the region.

A ruined Aeldari craftworld near the Maelstrom in Segmentum Ultima, once celebrated for artisanry before Slaanesh's birth shock tore the souls from its people, leaving only wreckage the Phoenix Lords still contest.

A Necron tomb world in Segmentum Ultima ruled by lords obsessed with reversing their transformation into living metal, harvesting captives for grim experiments under Illuminor Szeras's Cryptek conclave.
A Necron crown world of the Sarnekh Dynasty in Segmentum Pacificus, seized by the pirate phaeron Thaszar, who scavenges wrecks from an ancient War in Heaven battlefield still drifting in its orbit.

An Imperial agri and feudal world of the Coronid Deeps in Segmentum Obscurus, brought into Compliance during the Great Crusade yet slow to abandon its old ways, its fields prized for grox, krill and tharac wheat.
A war scarred world in Segmentum Obscurus whose single continent is crossed by city sized armored land vehicles, descendants of a lost colony whose rival nations never honored their peace treaty.
A rainforest shrouded forge world in Segmentum Tempestus settled during the Dark Age of Technology, once bound to the Thousand Sons and later reclaimed by the Imperium during the Great Scouring.
Once home to the Fire Hawks Space Marine Chapter in Segmentum Obscurus, burned lifeless by the Frateris Templars during the Wars of Apostasy in M36.
A jungle world in Segmentum Obscurus overrun by Hive Fleet Tiamet, its surface given over to psychic resonators that draw Genestealer pilgrims and were declared beyond Imperial reclamation.
